Could I knock at his door? Yes, but I would be afraid. His eyes are too...

### prAna303 — 2015-07-05T23:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-170969>

Love.

What is that thing called love? Why have we made it a collective meaning from it.

Today i see food in the supermarket framed and published just like a Facebook posting, it is there for presentation, a fashion or style, based upon the present trend. Most think that food with makeup is wonderful, fish is smelly.

Today i see tons of new generations take up smoking. It is the quick fix, the fast inhale that only takes you a minute, perfect to add to your full time work schedule. Can it be that working in a microwave makes them longing for enery bursts?

Today i see the men and women of politics attend to shows where they perform silly dances, just like the present viral trend on youtube, to sell themself for votes.

Today i see movies that is based upon the act of killing, hurting others, crying humans.

Today i see friends take drugs to make them stand the stress, to make their senses dull so they can relax.

But where do i see love?

Food should be the action of love sometimes. To honor the fisherman that went out on that rainy day, the winemaker that spends her days and nights, on the knees to observe the grapes. Turning the bottles with generations of wisdom, just because they feel they must...

Smoking can be, applied in a good place and time open doors to a place where plants were picked and fermented. You can eat a whole table of goodies with a single puff but it takes time from you. You can not stress a good smoke, it takes hours to travel with your senses, back and forth.

Dance not to collect votes, dance because you must... Taking music and motion is one of our best highs we can use. Even watching it makes you see something else inside of you, if your systems are at work you dance on the inside.

Work with your love and you will never be hold at prison. Drugs is of no use here because you do it for yourself and your pool of love.

Become a aficionado of life... Make potential from what is and open doors, remove limits and laws because it is time to pick up science to change what went wrong.

Every single day this idea becomes more true... From the show Lost and those who played with their minds on the meaning.

And for the love of women.

### caroline — 2015-07-06T10:33:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-171121>

Make potential from what is ........ truly great life lesson. Instead of wishing .... do.

what a wonderful mind picture .... a pool of love.

### b612 — 2015-07-28T17:42:48+0300

Hi.

I found this song today. It made me think of you. It reminds me of someone alone in the dark. At night, lots of stars and crazy cold. I would also add some husky dogs around but probably I prefer without.

It's written in Inuit language but I can't find the translation anywhere. All I know is that navvaatara means "I found it".

Anouri uqqusiju
Nipi tusaalirakku
Ilinnit ungagama
Inutuugumangilanga
Nallini qinirtara
Nalligijiqarlunga
Uimmagunnailunuk
Navvaasimalirannuk
Unnua ullungurtuq
Apirilaungatuq
Qaisimagiallaqujilutit
Suli saninniqujilutit
Navvaatara navvaatara

Found what? I really want to know.

### prAna303 — 2015-10-27T13:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-180548>

Mental Jet Lag.

When i think and make up a reality that is not in sync with the one i am located in, i am lost.

Today i can read about so many things that is going on around the corner of the world, i can make up some freaky shit about what my own reality is... The one source is modern technology, the one that has a focus on information. We blend the cocktail with gadgets and words without any connection.

Each time you look on your Facebook flow you loose the grip of your own environment, the things that show up has to sync with your own world but it does not and the price your cells will pay. Looking at a picture from a friend, far, far away makes your cells react. Together with the light it comes with you get changes that, at first is not seen but it can be sensed, keep doing it and you shut down the sense that has gotten the wrong information.

You loose the picture of what you are...

Fear comes together with this i think. We prepare ourselves to things that can not happen "live" and with this we limit what really can happen. We put away scary things just because it can do harm but why should it? And is that all bad?

Sometimes we get a glimse of life, quite often when we try something that is deadly, also known as being alive. Take a freedive, you slip down into water and let go. Trust that you are a part of water and you act and react without any lag, loose that focus for one second... Think about what you gonna eat for dinner and, shit, really bad shit can happen. Same goes with our ordinary daily life, think to much about what you will do later on and you get a Jet Lag, the mental one.

When i post this i loose my grip for a short period of time and if i do not compensate i will slip deeper down.

This is also why, i think. People who start to change for something different get a Jet Lag. The thing you are do not match your profile on Facebook anymore, why? One is real, the other a simulation. If you start to get well, say move from a bad place you still have to dig deeper into the other things that makes us living, it can be your style of fashion, your family and so many other things... For some this sounds like it will never end, i just wanna have some confort but that is the error we do make. Why is a life full of change bad? If it syncs with you and your environment it is like a walk in the park on a sunny day.

The world is not global in the way we use it today... It is all local but that local moment is connected to a greater big.

Now i close the lid on this piece of electronics and go out where time is not a factor only my response is important.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-10-27T14:44:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-180551>

Mental jet lag = cognitive haze = a new old fashion Rx = Sunrises and sunsets are clinics dispensing free care. Physics drives our biology and opens the door to the compound pharmacy in our pituitary. Every sunset is a reset, every sunrise collapses a new waveform. When you live in an optimized environment with ideal optics, most ailments can be cured; time can be slowed using light while surgery can repair the environment. Putting windex on your glass eye is brain surgery without a scalpel.

### prAna303 — 2015-10-29T17:34:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-180719>

Energy saver.

The mantra today is that we should save energy to save the earth, this goes with everything.

We are forced to buy shitty light for our homes, forced to produce work in a world built upon the cheapest supplier, from flooring to lighting.

We are forced to buy food that is based upon cheapest and the most effective producer.

We are forced to watch movies, sent to us on cheapest WiFi and crappy displays.

We get cars that do not pollute the world like the old ones, instead they are rolling cans of cancer.

I could go on forever and most people thing i am insane, buy and pay me free of my own thinking, yes Google aims for my brain...

Back to why and how we perhaps have gone insane, energy savers. We are living things that crave transfers of energy, our senses cost a lot and are made from nature to do some heavy duty living. Now is this why we are in love with technology? I mean, could the brain be slowing down just because it cost a lot to keep going, laying it in the cloud is so wonderful... The device to link external brain and us is modern technology and i think there can be good technology but today... Not sure. When i watch a movie on my iPhone i feel so good because i can relax, the brain can shut down parts that is no longer in need and therefor i save energy, big time. With this i also loose ability to read the environment, watching a lake on the latest TFT screen gives me signals that the cells can not understand, hey! Sun, lake, nude girls but i can not sense it with my skin, my smell, my ability to make contact... The mental Jet Lag.

This do not mean that i will go out and burn down each tree i see just because i need more walls to protect myself from the ugly nature.

Took a friend to a performance some time ago, he is hyper sensitive to everything that is the things we call art... He could almost cry seeing six humans on stage, dancing to live music, live classical music, handmade for the show. We sat upfront, having the complete setup in our knees... After something like this, how can you like Youtube?

If our brains were created last, or the most detailed systems were created not long ago, will these go first when we allow ourselves to put the brainpower on a external device? What happens when we no longer can respond like our living system is created?

Many, most... Think i am insane when i go in a t-shirt when it is -2C, you could catch a flu, i respond i think i have so i must chill i say. Watched some new monitors for movies today, i thought everything looked like shit, everyones looks like cancer i said, people just stared.

Life forms perhaps have a built in desire to save energy, save and live longer but when that response is made with nature as source, how could we get well today? Going into a sick place (read manmade building) to take a test will always give a sick answer... If you base your theory on a sick basis you tell everyone they are sick because each of us respond very different but the laws is based on nature. Give me a doctor that takes the ill ones on a trip to a deep forest for a week and look how they respond and LOOK, hey... Is this Doctor 3.5?

Enough! Have some strings to listen to, also some sexy female violinist...

### caroline — 2015-10-30T00:55:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-180747>

I am sitting in our backyard - on a hill ....and all I can see is miles of all different kinds of tree tops and flowering shrubs in purples and reds ...and a small mountain as a backdrop.

The sky is very blue, the grass is very green, birds are singing, the ocean is 10 minutes away ....and I couldn't be happier and more peaceful...

Should I be doing stuff - of course ..... but, right now this is the most important place in the world to me.....

### prAna303 — 2015-11-16T20:26:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-182086>

When shit hits the fan... Or?

We are designed for usage of one sun and some plenty of stars, stars that can and will walk among us humans.

Do you use a lightsource that tells you a story or do you look upon the world where everything is lit by sources? Since the very day that we walked away from nature we have given ourselves versions of life, a little less that the real thing.

Many things fell into place when viewing some clips a couple of weeks ago... One one side i had a regular 85 inch tv-set pounding out information, above me to the other i had a projector blasting the very same story but what a difference. Light straight into all my senses makes me feel, fucked up. Light painted on a screen of silver made me take a trip...

When i see the world now and it has changed in many ways the last months, i love the idea of a stage. On that stage everything can be lit by a can of light but the person watching will also see much more, shadows, blur and many other things. Sound can be there so you must react, feel something that goes into that world you sit and observe... The whole place is black, you go into a dreamstate or is it back to reality?

Someone who is "on top" can send so many signals. Someone who is real will make many look but not many will have the power to meet the glance, why? The potential of someone with a strong flow will make the weak ones look away... Just like staring into the sun if you are weak.

I have meet these ones, the above ones and i can only say, wow.

Today i feel something very new to me... I have a feeling of loss and that one is great. This burning sensation deep inside steers my destination and i have a thirst. Just like those vampires we have seen on movies for a long time i am hungry, that sensation when you drink life is so hunting. I talked to someone about this feeling and now i know that this one can kill, break and shake you down that rabbit hole.

Now that one project has come to an end, almost i feel a hunger. I can not go back, never. Today i understand why an actor will search for kicks, a rock star dig into drugs and many other seek destroy... The world we make when we create, act, dance and many other things is our own and when we are forced into society we crash, we long into a dreamworld where everything is possible, this i must search now.

So in the end, what light will you be? The one that shines, the one that is lit or just the one hunked down against a screen of simulation.

### prAna303 — 2015-11-16T20:34:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-182088>

Full range...

From this very day i must give myself a deal, i need the full spectrum no matter what.

When i listen to music, bang on my whole body. Earphones gives me just some small part of it, blasting it out with real sound, yummy.

When i eat i go into full range, no simple fake product, give me that fishy thing now, the whole beast.

When i meet people, stop and drop... That fucking iPhone, if you do not i turn the other side and search further.

When i walk... I walk!

When i stare into the sun i do it live and direct, no hanky panky, no mumbo jumbo, just the real deal... Sun and living humans (and other living things).

We must stop using the limited spectrum, we are not designed to just think we feel it, we need to use every sense.

Also, perhaps all matter is light collected and held together... Until we know the other way for sure i will only let as many as possible of good waves hit me, period.

After seeing this performance, live with live music, this made me make above promise.

### prAna303 — 2015-11-19T17:33:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-182347>

Slave to love.

Do parents take care of us still after many long years? Have you left the nest yet or do you blame DNA.

If you learn by systems based upon your parents, their versions of life you also end up living your parents life. Quite early on i think we loose the ability to just play around, solve problems without any hesitation, play is love.

Same thing goes when we live the same day over and over again or think that that is what we need. Work of today is more deadly than before, the free will of workers is an illusion. Some make it, they are often gurus that change things... Everyone can do this!

After some days with some actors we played the game of masks... Some had great problems to become that mask, others were great. Scaling it up and i see what i want in life. You can do everything because you take what you find and create. Same with music i think. Play by the notes and some will shine but many must shut down their own world just because we made laws.

I jumped the nest many years ago and did it again yesterday.

### prAna303 — 2015-11-19T18:18:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-182352>

Snow and ice.

Here it comes, first white flings today, made me think.

Often one can feel that this darkness is demanding and then this white gold comes and light up my day.

Is snow sunlight collected?

When everything is clean and white, the small amount of light i have shines like hell. I see it everywhere, even in the dark, snow.

Might be wrong but i like the idea...

### prAna303 — 2015-11-20T10:14:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-182411>

Red light, go!

The remains of the fire is still glowing...

I think that the idea that cooking food made us great is under attack. Can it be that the real action took place in the fire?

To make food warm just makes it a comfort thing, we looked upon the fire and felt good, cold nights it gave us warmth and we started to think, warm is good. Lets make a stew...

Since we came from the sea we started a reaction that has nothing with food to do, it was a light thing. Sun during the days we knew but when we let it go also at night, we got some extra information for the system to take care of, we got smarter. That red light has been with us all the time since then, we love it. Why do some creators dream in lucid red?

That fire made food function but not by blasting it into a mess.

Us humas have had some deep love affairs with these two sources, sun and fire, guess why many love to burn stuff? We want to take back life into our own world again, shit happens when you are a mess for sure, burning homes may not be good but many should indeed.

Fire, walk with me.

### prAna303 — 2015-12-05T15:10:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-183362>

Your own personal cannibal.

When we get sick we share light.

When we are on top of the world we share light.

The difference can be just a tiny fabric, just showing some skin makes me go crazy, in a good way.

I think we must not just focus on light that hits us but also the light that should be dropped. Today we eat the light that we should leave behind and the bad things goes back into us. We were not made to suck on old lollipops...

Lets say, i get a flu. My unit of flesh and other things goes warm, and warm we know is light. This light must be dropped and not kept, no way mama i will wear a hat when cold. If we bunk up on clothes because we get shivers we block and would perhaps even create some bad new lights. Just like a wave of sound can have effects on matter, light will. Also the colour as we know do matter. This kind of distortion should not happen, we need clean sources.

This would also be a good reason why aluminum sucks, used the wrong way.

When i sleep and someone next to me can eat me, good. But when i eat myself, shit happens.

### prAna303 — 2015-12-23T16:01:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-184829>

At the age of seven.

My life changes every single second, my parts that is me is also changed. Those parts must come and go, good times and bad.

With a glimpse in my rear mirror i see constructions that i once was, paths that lead me here and soon, very soon my slaughter must begin once again. Cells come and go, trying to keep them for as long as possible would only slow me down, drag me to the dark side...

Each seven years i felt a new person framing inside of me and with this i dropped so many bad things, just like the skin on the snake, left behind like a memory. To cling on, to obtain what was will make me ill. Perhaps the cure for cancer is to get a life again. We put lotion on dry skin to just keep it, just like it once was. Maybe those parts must dry off and fall to the earth once again so that we can make new and fresh stories.

To look upon life from a scale of time passing by, from start to end is a dead end. In my life i die and become new, again and again.

The light will soon come back but for many it will not. Living is being alive and letting things go on without any filters, and filters is manmade. If you use a smartphone it is only smart at being a fool, it gives you an illusion of things, distorted and fucked up. So perhaps this is some kind of goodbye, or at least a turn back. Technology can be good but until that day, i turn to real life...

My acting has taken me to worlds that i make, my breath training puts me into states where my body goes to repair and my mind travels thru space and time, from girl to girl, from dreams to reality.

When i lay my hands on this keyboard i feel distorted, just like some ill tuned instrument and all my compass needles turn the wrong way, is the solution to accept and like it? Or is it to limit it...

I fixed one of my last injuries with help of you, Jack. Like turbo speed i broke up my bad patterns, my limited shoulder and made a new one from the memories that these cells carry. Now i must change the rest of my life, with more passion.

### caroline — 2015-12-24T10:01:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-184860>

So much passion Peter ..... You are magic!

You never stop evolving .... I can't wait to see where this next year takes you, what shapes you, what touches you, what twists and turns in your universe tempts you, what makes you sad, what makes you happy, what makes you laugh, what makes you cry, what empowers you to new heights .....

### prAna303 — 2015-12-25T11:23:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-new-old-fashioned.6069/post-184925>

Have you heard the news, the circus is in town.

From this day on i live a circus life, why?

Nobody can go to the circus and not feel, if you say you do not, you lie. Since you say that, you feel, may it be passion, hate, anger, fear or just something unknown. When you buy the ticket you also start to build up feeling, what is going on inside that place, that arena, you feel the smell of popcorn bathing in butter, kids start to run around, jumping in place, there is something in the air tonight...

Everyone in this world, the circus world wants and need to be special, original and a freak. You may learn basic movement, basic skills but to give something to others, you need to stand out, simply be YOU!

Crimping your hand against the seat, holding your friends hand when you see the girl flying you balance between fear, risk and hope. Something inside of you snap, you turn away from your ordinary life and you are the one flying, we mirror because we are living things. Sometimes they fail, some get hurt or even die but how did they fail? With style, grace, passion and love.

They say that you have it or not, the heart of circus... I think we all do because the circus gives back what we miss in modern world. When you see some kind of performance, sports, dance or many other things you are in awe. Circus makes you wanna do, try and shine a little. The circus is the alternative to society in many ways...

Mostly, it is not really static. You travel and life changes, what you did yesterday is not that important, you have to adapt to now, otherwise the price is quite high, many times... Death. All of us is a new person today, another tomorrow and on and on. We build that act so we can be unique, rare, scaring or sexy.

Compare the circus with daytime or nighttime work these days. We are returning to a place that is static, humans that is limited by laws, systems that is made from ones and zeros, we even look into fake suns that is not original, for sure. Most often we are shut down because you could not have a freaking group of originals claiming their place and possibility to perform... When we go to the party with these people, some wine can drag the clown, the magician and other stuff to the stage... Have you not felt this?

The work, backstage is demanding, painful, scary... Conflict is present all the time, injuries also but you accept that this is life and we go on. We live to be alive with others, we make a difference to each single one in the crowd. Someone will connect to you, removing the obstacle that blocking you endless possibilities. Why should someone in a wheelchair be dumbed down like we do today and make the world more friendly to them? Here in the circus, you shine on and make it a part of YOU! All of us can contribute to the circus...

When you live a life, having a thought that being connected is good only, then you have to select with great skills.

Since we should catch the ray of light during daytime we share it tonight at the show. We connect in the circus because we are all part of a group of humans that is driven by the love of life. Here we do not go to work, we are the work. We do not leave family behind for endless hours, they are part of the show. Perhaps your wife is your partner in the show, maybe she is solo... Your kids do not go to school that is dumbed down and numb to feelings, they learn and evolve with humans nearby. Starting to see why so many of us, once did have a feeling that... I WANT TO RUN OF WITH THE CIRCUS.

Small kids of today get into the feed early, one year old ones love the smartphone in the cafe when mommy and daddy shares their snack on Facebook. The shit hits the fan when you remove the gadget, crying, screaming and kicking starts and it all ends in a mess. The same happens to you and me BUT we have put personality upon us so we shut down the response, who wanna cry in fear at the cafe? I think that the brain can be good at sensing things but what is reality. The kid wanders into a fake sun projected straight into the eyes and this makes you snowblind, turn of the sun and you die, you snap back into a real world and this one is not that wonderful because we created it with bad taste. Who would not be scared if you did not see the sun tomorrow? Oh, back to instagram, those wonderful flowers... Aah! I am alive (illusion).

The light shines on the current show going on tonight. We sit in darkness so we can focus on seeing what we once was and if you are smart, you better run of with that girl that made you squirm in your seat. Do not just take her home like a puppy, you join HER world.

### Jude — 2013-02-27T02:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ramifications-of-a-calcified-pineal.6077/post-77927>

Here's an excerpt from Wickipedia

"The human pineal gland grows in size until about 1â€“2 years of age, remaining stable thereafter,\[17\]\[18\] although its weight increases gradually from puberty onwards.\[19\]\[20\] **The abundant melatonin levels in children are believed to inhibit sexual development**, and pineal tumors have been linked with precocious puberty. **When puberty arrives, melatonin production is reduced.**
Calcification of the pineal gland is typical in adults, and has been observed in children as young as 2. Calcification rates vary widely by country and tend to increase by age, with **calcification occurring in an estimated 40% of Americans by their 17th year.**\[4\] Calcification of the pineal gland is largely associated with corpora arenacea (or "acervuli," or "brain sand").
Apparently the internal secretions of the pineal gland inhibit the development of the reproductive glands, because in cases where it is severely damaged in children, the result is accelerated development of the sexual organs and the skeleton.\[2"

Just following up an ask Jack question..found a few questions....like
Are sleep issues hard to fix if pineal is calcified? How do you diagnose?
40% Americans by their 17th yr? wow.
This seems pretty important given the role of melatonin.
Someone mentioned somewhere that their neice had pubic hair at eight?
Just wondered if it's to do with a calcified pineal?
I suppose it's other expression of the all encompassing entanglement we're all in?

### Jack Kruse — 2015-04-27T20:20:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ramifications-of-a-calcified-pineal.6077/post-162566>

Melatonin is filled with an aromatic ring. Your pictogram shows it in this blog. What does this mean? Only a few biomolecules that are good conductors. These tend to have aromatic rings, such as most of the hormones like melatonin, testosterone and progesterone. Recall that leptin action controls the release of all of these quantum critical biomolecules. Being quantum critical means the protein can control electrons in altered fields. Most of the molecules taking part actively in biochemical processes are tuned exactly to the transition point and are critical conductors for this very specific reason. The permutations of possible energy levels of biomolecules is huge so the possibility of finding even one that is in the quantum critical state by accident is mind-bogglingly small. The EM spectrum has 10 to the 73rd possible frequencies to control proteins but it turns out there are only 1,000,000 chemicals on Earth that have these electronic states with these abilities. These are the chemicals that the 3 kingdoms of life organize around. Once you focus your attention on their abilities you begin to see how the cosmic wand organized life at the most fundamental level. It is all about energy and energy states and charge. The electromagnetic spectrum is design to move charges of atoms or ions to give it a control feature. The innate fact that the electromagnetic force can exquisitely control electrons in proteins gives them the quantum critical features to innovate life from energy. The behavior of electrons in large molecules when they occupy the exotic state that sits at the knife’s edge between conduction and insulation is the critical piece, and it is the aromatic ring is one of the molecular footprints that point out why hormones all have it. Vitamin D3 and Vitamin A have it as well. And this explains why VDR and RXR work precisely with DHA. DHA can turn the suns light into an electric field to control the flow of these electrons. The electrons in biomolecule must be kept in in a state between freedom and control in the lattice. The atomic lattice configuration then sets the Rosetta stone control of incident light frequency that moves of holds the electrons to carry out physiologic abilities. When these molecules are conductors, some of their bound electrons are able to move freely under the influence of an electric field. By contrast, when these molecules are insulators, the electrons are not free to move and can withstand changes to environmental electric fields. The key to understand how they react to magnetic field alterations. These field alter electron spin…….and that does not require any movement. I have a deep sense this is where modern communication is affecting our quantum critical proteins in a deep unyielding fashion. We do not understand this connection in modern biology. We need to.

Vitamin D3 and Vitamin A have it as well. And this explains why VDR and RXR work precisely with DHA. DHA can turn the suns light into an electric field to control the flow of these electrons. The key to understand how they react to magnetic field alterations. These field alter electron spin…….and that does not require any movement. I have a deep sense this is where modern communication is affecting our quantum critical proteins in a deep unyielding fashion. We do not understand this connection in modern biology. We need to.

Important points IMO:

\- **All cyclic compounds only exhibit conformational isomerism** -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conformational\_isomerism](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conformational_isomerism) . if I'm not wrong, such compounds are not altered by light. Constrast this to compounds like L-Cysteine, which can be converetd to D-Cysteine by light.

As you'd expect, molecules like melatonin and testosterone are very flat and pretty small, and can only fit to a receptor in relatively few different ways (modulated by only a few quantised energetic levels ... see next point). This is not some big protein signalling molecule like insulin, whereby protein folding determines a lot of it's function.

\- **All aromatic ring compounds will have a fixed number of delocalised pi-electrons**, forming a pi-electron cloud just like DHA. (Huckel's Rule -- [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hückel's\_rule](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/H%C3%BCckel%27s_rule)) This rule states that there will be (4n + 2) delocalised pi-electrons, where n is the set of integers \[0, 6\]. ie: There will either be 2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, or 26 pi-electrons

**All atoms within such a compound can interact with the pi-electron cloud**, which will allow for modulation of the n-value. ie: Melatonin can take different n-values and thus have a different number of delocalised pi-electrons, depending on the energy levels of the environment it finds itself in.

\-----

To me, this is just yet another class of signalling molecule that nature happened to come up with.

Some compounds rely primarily on more macro 3D shapes for modulating their interaction with other objects, and small compounds like melatonin try to "control for all other variables" except for excitation energy levels.

\----

Melatonin fits into the Ubiquitination picture, since low melatonin signals an excess energy state which "needs to be quelled" before some other processes are "safe to continue with" (those downstream of prolactin).

Again, any impairment of melatonin-to-pineal interaction is another loss of negative feedback, which the body interprets as a chronic higher energy state. Ubiquitination rates nede to go up to calm things down.

....

# Thread: More bone loss in space
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-bone-loss-in-space.6081/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike NYC — 2013-02-27T05:38:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-bone-loss-in-space.6081/post-77949>

I found this last month writing a small article about how structure creates function in movement for a course I am doing (EMF2 made me look for a space example I could use).
Its about bone loss in space in spite of exercise using resistance training and simulated "gravity" provided by bungee cords on a treadmill. Key point - the cosmonauts lost more bone and lean tissue than the "control" group which was patients on 4 months of bed rest on earth with no exercise!
[http://www.ismni.org/jmni/pdf/2/leblanc.pdf](http://www.ismni.org/jmni/pdf/2/leblanc.pdf)

# Thread: EMF and Neuroplasticity?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-neuroplasticity.6082/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mike NYC — 2013-02-27T05:53:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-neuroplasticity.6082/post-77955>

I wrote this on Saturday while about halfway through EMF 7, but I got a message that it needed to be approved by a moderator and it never showed up, possibly because I had a few links in it as well. I'm about halfway through EMF 8 at the moment now, but Jack's comment on the Q&A about waking up in the middle of the night to write a post reminded me about how I felt on Sat when I needed to share some thoughts in my head before I could focus on some work.
Anyway, as I read more and my thoughts continue to evolve, it seems like the quantum effects likely dwarf anything else in our function, or maybe that is even a big part of how neuroplasticity works... but I feel like I had something that may be worthwhile to share in that post, so I'm going to to post it again as it was written on Sat.

So I'm about halfway through EMF 7, just re-read the food electrons blog, and I need to take a break and do some other work before I spend my whole weekend reading, but I have so many thoughts going on in my mind that I feel like I need to share.

Since EMF 5 & 6, specifically the paper and the video showing how EMF radiation changes glucose metabolism in neurons and how dark neurons developed in mice after EMF exposures, I have been wondering about neuroplasticity, specifically metaplasticity as in the work of Jeffrey Kleim. It seems to me that the more plastic we can keep our brains and the more plastic and flexible we are able to maintain the ability of our brains to continually rewire quickly and efficiently will help us to continue optimal function using non-damaged neurons to create connections that take over for connections involving a damaged one. In this way it seems that the better our cells can signal with one another, the better they can modulate that signal as needed as well. Perhaps they may even be able to signal in networks that have some sort of quantum quality where parts of the network can be effectively "off" and "on" at the same time? That last part is a bit of a random thought, and I don't have a great handle on quantum theory, but I'm just thinking out loud.

It does seem to me that the better we are at expressing this neuro and meta-plasticity , the better we will be able to navigate the problems modern life throws our way at the neurological and cellular level. This is why continued learning, novel movement, and providing the brain and body proper fuel and activation are essential for life.

If chronic pain can both create cortical plastic changes and can also alter DNA expression; but through movement, mirror box therapies, and other works of Ramachandran and Moseley, we can create cortical plastic change in the other direction that improves the pain, that would change DNA as well. Furthermore, thought, especially conscious critical thinking against the grain seems to require plasticity and an ability to change (dopamine). I think I am really seeing how true that "a thought can change your DNA" quote really is.

It also seems to me that environmental/evolutionary mismatch is a type of sensory mismatch (analogous to Visual Vestibular Proprioception integration problems), then EV mismatch is also likely a chronic nociceptive stimulus to the nervous system and the body.... however it may not have the amplitude to cause an acute pain experience and it takes time to develop into neolithic disease conditions.

Then I saw this post about quantum entanglement - [http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5786-Quantum-Entanglement](http://jackkruse.com/forum/showthread.php?5786-Quantum-Entanglement)
That to me says something of the power of this metaplasticity expanded to a society, and type of uber-plasticity maybe, and the role of the starfish in being the change you hope to see in others... and the more people that make positive changes, the faster that change can spread. For a while the EMF series was making me rather sad when I thought about the future, but looking at it this way I see the potential for it to be bright. Though the path is not without challenges.

One thing I do know is that whenever I work with someone as a training client, after asking what their goals are and finding ways I can help them to achieve them, I always ask myself how what I am doing with them is affecting their brain and how their brain is affecting their movement/workout and the change they desire to make. Over the last 3 years or so, neuroscience and neurology has become a huge part of how I have approached physical training, and what I have learned on this blog over the last 1.5 years have had an incredible impact on how I view all of that.

This has been a bit of a ramble, and I may not be correct in my understanding of everything, but that it how it is appearing to me at the moment.
Anyway, signing off as I need to do some work so I can get back to EMF 7 and checking out Jack's book.... if I didn't get these thoughts out of my head I never would have been able to focus on other stuff....

### Jack Kruse — 2013-02-27T14:29:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-neuroplasticity.6082/post-77993>

goog stuff here........Mike. If you call it a ramble we need more of your Rambles.

I am committed to change that is for sure but I have tried so many things you can't imagine!!! From Macrobiotic , to Vegan , to the PH Miracle the list goes on and on. My mother died of Breast CA at 53. This year I turned 54 and just consider every year a gift but I so much want to live to a healthy ripe old age!! I think I've found the right place.

Candace

# Thread: Cave Explorer Hunts Antibiotics 1,600 Feet Down
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cave-explorer-hunts-antibiotics-1-600-feet-down.6090/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-02-27T17:30:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cave-explorer-hunts-antibiotics-1-600-feet-down.6090/post-78015>

[http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2013-02-27/cave-explorer-hunts-antibiotics-1-600-feet-down-health.html](http://www.bloomberg.com/news/2013-02-27/cave-explorer-hunts-antibiotics-1-600-feet-down-health.html)

In a remote cave 1,600 feet below New Mexicoâ€™s Carlsbad Caverns National Park, Hazel Barton, a microbiologist working with Cubist Pharmaceuticals Inc. (CBST), has found a drug-resistant bacteria that may one day help extend the life of Cubistâ€™s best-selling antibiotic, Cubicin.

As bacteria develop resistance to available medicines, modern methods of antibiotic development in labs have failed to keep pace with an emerging crop of superbugs. Many drugmakers have dropped of the market because of regulatory hurdles and the poor return on investment.

That failure carries a steep price. Antibiotic-resistant infections are estimated to add $21 billion to $34 billion in costs to the U.S. health-care system each year, according to the World Economic Forumâ€™s report. **Antibiotic-resistant Bacteria cause the majority of the 99,000 deaths annually in North America from infections acquired in hospitals**, the report said.

### lioness7 — 2013-02-27T22:27:33+0200

This thread made laugh so hard....too bad it wasn't around during my son's txt. He developed ringworm which was misdiagnosed for months as eczema...i disagreed and started treating him myself for ringworm...of course being on chemo and steroids and time most my home txt didnt cure but kept it from spreading and it began to regress but would flare up after the five day steroid protocol. It was a hard balancing act. His doctor was not too thrilled to give lamasil pills due to its toxic effect on the liver nor was i willing to stick another drug into him. I tried everything including colloidal silver....that was great stuff. But when they found out i was applying it within a few hours i received a phone call from the onc pharmacist to immediately cease and desist its use as it would turn him blue!..... Seriously? Did she think i was DIY ing it. When i mentioned that it is used in the burn ward she had no response. One too many Oprah shows....the pharmacist should have scrolled down past the first hit. Gosh i wonder what she would have said about DMSO use? The prescribed creams did nothing.....i had no choice at that moment. It is great stuff. Just make sure you have a top grade product. Successful with all my kids for sore throats cuts etc

### caroline — 2013-02-28T04:51:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-candace-in-massachusetts.6096/post-78242>

Okay - I think there is so much information here it is kind of confusing! Like I said - if you go to the home page - you will see the blog index on the right. So start with the one that Dr. k recommends for you. Personally - if I had to do it all over again - I would become a silver or gold member depending on finances. A Gold member gets the webinars and Q&A included and reduced costs for consults so it really is the most cost effective. It seems like you have a lot of stuff to solve so I would do an email consult with Dr. Kruse and he will tell you what labs he wants you to have and then do a consult when you get your labs back.
I think that gets you started on the right foot - I wish I had done that.

Start the leptin reset and follow it exactly - some say to start slowly - but honestly - I think it is way better to just jump in and do it to the letter. You want to get to optimum and there are no short cuts! and you want an A+ not a B

start CT - there is a guide for that. Don't underestimate the importance of this and don't be a wimp - just do it! if you live near an ocean - as Dr. K. says - you are a damn fool if you don't get in it every day! grounding is extremely important - electrons

Light cycles are extremely important - maybe the most important - for me they certainly are.

Sooooo - start with what Dr. Kruse said ....start the reset ... start CT and pay extreme attention to artificial light.

come back and ask questions .... there are no stupid questions .....honestly - we have all been there!

### ashryn — 2013-02-28T14:04:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-candace-in-massachusetts.6096/post-78283>

Hi candice, welcome
I'm going to throw my two cents in here and say that even though all the things Caroline said are true and important, you need to start somewhere, and I reckon if you try to do everything at once it will be overwhelming. Not to mention the possibility of the detoxing that might happen and the carb flu making it all too hard to continue.. So fwiw, I think start with the leptin reset. It's pretty easy, and is a quick win because you'll feel good on it within weeks if not days. Whilst you're getting started on that, start reading about cold thermogenesis (CT) find it in the blog index or search the home page (you get to that via a link at the bottom right of the forum screen.. Click on jack kruze)

theres a great blog post called sunshine of your life too which is one of the first ones you should read..
If you find my journal, I have summarised a few of the blog posts to help remind me of the basics for when I forget.. It might help you.

As you read a blog come back into the forum and find the one related to the blog you just read for the answers to all the questions you will have.. Lots of people have asked them and it really helped me to read the comments and forum posts after reading the blogs.

I predict that you're going to freak when you read some of the odd things people are doing.. (Wait till you work out what a MHS is.. And I'm not telling!) you will most likely be reading incredulously and thinking you'd never do that, because its so extreme... Then before you know it you're putting ice in your pants and wallpapering with aluminium foil! Or something ;P

good luck on your journey.. It probably doesn't really matter where you start it, so long as you do

### IceBarbie — 2013-03-01T05:18:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-candace-in-massachusetts.6096/post-78457>

When I started using T3, my fibromyalgia went away. Completely. One study showed that 85% of women with FMS had an underlying metabolic disorder related to the thyroid. Thankfully I was one of the 85%.

Many people with Hashis stop eating gluten-containing grains as this seems to cause or worsen the autoimmune problem. Any food you are allergic to can aggravate hashimotos. The fun part is figuring out which foods affect you.

It took me 4 months on the LeptinRX before I felt any changes. My insomnia improved. Except the weight just kept creeping up while following the protocol. But I sure learned how to eat breakfast for the first time in my life!

CT (Cold Therapy) made my thighs even fatter. Perhaps your experience will be different.

You must be overwhelmed at the replies you've gotten here. My heart goes out to you! I wish you well.

# Thread: Damn it, Jim, without this Tricorder, you're flying blind!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Melinda — 2013-02-28T02:56:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/post-78195>

Got our new EMF tester from lessemf.com: Trifield extended range ~$190, I thought I'd give a report. I hope this saves someone the expense.

OMG, the nastiest place in the house is the kitchen and the microwave is now shut off at the circuit breaker. Mine is built-in above the cook top and even not running it emits in the un-safe range out about 3 feet. When it's running, the needle is buried out to about 6 feet. My appliances are 6-7 years old, ymmv. The refrig needs a bubble of about 3 feet when it cycles on only. The biggest surprise was the Cuisinart food processor emits out 1-2 feet even when not running, simply unplugging it solves that problem. The wall mounted oven emits out to 3 feet when running only. Dimmer switches and variable fan switches emit out 1 foot when running only so don't lean you head against the wall in the water closet. Our gizmos are OK except my Tracfone when on a call, I need to hold it about arms length to get the needle to drop.

One more kitchen item, the Costco power wine bottle opener: just sitting and charging it reaches just past the counter edge, I'm going back to brute force.

That's all I can think of right now.

My husband says he bought it through Amazon for ~$150 delivered. Sorry about that, foggy head.

I didn't know there were power wine bottle openers. But then, what isn't available in a power option. LOL.

Have you tested anything around/in your car?? I get super tired when I drive long distances along the big NE cities...just wondering....

### RickD — 2013-02-28T04:37:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/post-78235>

I picked up my meter last week and the results have been very illuminating. I split my time between the Mts. in British Columbia and Calgary and after picking up the meter I checked out the Mt. house and the readings looked really good, no real trouble spots. Bringing it with me to Calgary I was shocked to find I live in a magnetic field Chernobyl. Appears the water pipe in the basement isn't properly grounded and readings in the house range from a low of 15 mg to a pegged meter. Out back the garage is detached and above it are some typical residential style power lines. Here the meter is pegged throughout the garage. With the data in hand it now makes sense that after keeping my CRP below .3 the last few years it suddenly jumped to .6 over 6 months. I also find myself more irritable in Calgary since the move which I was attributing to the longer commute, but now thinking maybe it's the house.

I have a Magnetico 20 gauss pad on the bed and a grounding sheet so I'm hoping that will offset some of the problem until I get the water line repaired.

### Melinda — 2013-02-28T04:50:26+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/post-78243>

Rick - Jack mentioned something about oil impacting the earth's magnetic field. That may be the problem with Calgary. And you're not the first person that I've heard say that they feel more irritable in Calgary.

Oh my - if I got one of those meters I would never put it down! I'd probably point it at people on the street I'd get so obsessed LOL.

### RickD — 2013-02-28T04:56:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/post-78245>

### RickD — 2013-02-28T05:24:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/damn-it-jim-without-this-tricorder-youre-flying-blind.6103/post-78250>

### lioness7 — 2013-02-28T07:59:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-78264>

I replaced my bedrooms end table lights with the bulbs from lowbluelight.com.....must say quite relaxing when i walk in..older kids said it looked creeepy...i think it looks like a 19th century bordello....well that could be good ....lol
.... Luckily my bathroom has two light switches so i replaced one fixture with the amber bulb....love it.....next phase when i find the ladder all bedroom ceiling lamps will get the same txt. I wear my blue blockers doing laundry cuz some a\* hole electrician decided on my behalf that halogen potlights were the way to go......i hate them and did not notice how bad they were til i put the blue block glasses on....what relief!....like a veil lifted off. I hate the new lights which require an electrical engineering degree to purchase the right series or replace....have been stockpiling the old bulbs for years....requires lots of $$$$ to replace existing fixtures that require those high tech bulbs....but my laundry room will be first as i spend the most time in there (three washers and dryers so imagine)...dh has purchased a huge beeswax candle to augment the amber light as he loves to read before sleeping ...he is not quite on board but appreciates what i say though reticent.. but pliable
I have researched and tried to find amber glass reflector replacements for the halogens....came up with one site but lost it.....one of those designer sites....cheaper than changing fixtures and drywall....anyone with any input?

### BJK77 — 2013-02-28T13:46:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-78281>

I found amber bulbs at my local mom-and-pop hardware store. I'd rather frequent places like that than HD any day!

### nonchalant — 2013-02-28T16:35:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-78303>

DH objected at first when I installed dim or yellow bulbs and started turning off lights after dark. He really couldn't see at all. But with time, and some diet changes, he is seeing better and better. I recently changed the bulb in the refrigerator from a 60 watt bug light to a 40 watt amber light, and he didn't even notice. And it has made me a lot happier, because I don't have to squint when I open it any more, even wearing my red glasses that block more light than the orange ones do.

Similarly, the orange goggles made things quite dark for me at first, and it was difficult to read the computer screen with them. But I adjusted, and then looked for darker lenses. The red goggles were too dark at first also, but I've adapted nicely.

However, I have a small fridge that I keep my water bottles in, and I access that in the evenings. Wish it had a conventional-sized bulb. Hmm, maybe I could paint over the clear plastic that covers the bulb...

### Claudia — 2013-02-28T22:15:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-78383>

Are these the right bulbs for just around the house? [http://www.amazon.com/60-Watt-Ceram...=1362082104&sr=1-15&keywords=amber+light+bulb](http://www.amazon.com/60-Watt-Ceramic-Amber-Incandescent-Medium/dp/B009RTB2QQ/ref=sr_1_15?s=hi&ie=UTF8&qid=1362082104&sr=1-15&keywords=amber+light+bulb)

My H is very resistant to all this. It's funny but I used to wonder why he was always in the dark and I called him a vampire....going back before kids now, so 10 or so years ago. I wanted the lights on so I could eat and see and he would shut them off and could eat with minimal light. Now we have switched places. I can't stand when he walks in from work at 9:30 and turns on all the lights that I have dimmed. He insists that he can't see but he is having trouble with his eyes lately and needs to go to the eye doctor, probably needs glasses.

Silly question: Can I still watch a little tv with the orange glasses on? I usually do watch a movie with my H on the weekends when the kids go to sleep. That's part of our time together without kids.

### diane — 2013-02-28T23:33:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-78402>

Claudia - mine has the TV on most of the time, so I use the glasses. He loves the lights on too, but at least now all the lights are off even though the TV is on. It's all a work in progress.

### Claudia — 2013-03-07T03:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-79413>

Also, got my orange glasses and have been wearing them at sundown. They are not that comfortable though. Bother me around the ears, in fact that's why I gave up glasses for contact lenses so long ago. Also, if I"m reading in bed, is it ok to just read with my amber light on or do I need to wear the orange glasses, too? What is the bedtime routine like for you with glasses on? I find it hard to brush my teeth, but even worse is to take off makeup with glasses on. Pretty much impossible so I'm doing it in the dark and not doing a very good job of it, lol. I take my contact lenses off in the dark and then, put my eyeglasses on under the orange glasses, not so comfortable either. Hopefully I'll get used to it more. My H is also getting used to this change and things I'm sooooo weird .

### diane — 2013-03-07T03:57:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-79416>

### lioness7 — 2013-03-07T05:04:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-79427>

> [Claudia said:](/goto/post?id=79413)
>

Bother me around the ears, in fact that's why I gave up glasses for contact lenses so long ago. Also, if I"m reading in bed, is it ok to just read with my amber light on or do I need to wear the orange glasses, too? What is the bedtime routine like for you with glasses on? I find it hard to brush my teeth, but even worse is to take off makeup with glasses on. Pretty much impossible so I'm doing it in the dark and not doing a very good job of it, lol. I take my contact lenses off in the dark and then, put my eyeglasses on under the orange glasses, not so comfortable either. Hopefully I'll get used to it more. My H is also getting used to this change and things I'm sooooo weird .

Claudia...weird is cool. Last year when i got my blockers my family had a field day with me. I took them to Europe in the fall and have sent several pairs to them as per their request (must be in the genes) I too wear contacts. My glasses broke a few years back and i never replaced them. It works out good because if I don't have to leave the house I wear no contacts. The plus is that with a rx of 3.50 my vision is good enough to do my housework but not good enough to see the finer messes of fingerprints et c that would drive the clean freak part of me nuts....what i don't see doesn't bother me.....quite therapeutic. I don't like wearing the contacts more than i have to as at 50 I am getting a bit farsighted. I find the loss of close vision aqcuity bothers me more than distance loss. The rx for distance has not changed since I was 18. I read sometimes before I go to sleep with either the yellow bulbs with or witbout the amber glasses. I have cocoons...they block 100% blue light....still looking into green wavelength. 29.95 on their site and i find them pretty comfortable...free shipping code 2020.....ordered more for the kids online since the darn optometry place charged me $70...ugh.. love how relaxed my eyes and mind feel when I wear them. Contact lens wearers should remove their lenses at least an hour before going to bed / insert at least half an hour to an hour after waking....if not you risk varicose type veins developing from prolonged use cutting off needed oxygen to your eyes. (Actually, further info, my sister did the eye exercises from a few books i gave her which I was too busy to do and reduced her prescription by a whole diopter, her optometrist could't believe it and retested twice)

Anyways I guess none of this would be of concern if we could get to bed earlier......lookin' forward to the next power outage.....agh, that will only work if I disconnect the back up generator!....oh and of course, get off the darn computer....but then again how would we get all of Dr. K's fabulous information???!!!!

I find it hard to brush my teeth, but even worse is to take off makeup with glasses on. Pretty much impossible so I'm doing it in the dark and not doing a very good job of it, lol. I take my contact lenses off in the dark and then, put my eyeglasses on under the orange glasses, not so comfortable either. Hopefully I'll get used to it more. My H is also getting used to this change and things I'm sooooo weird .

I don't think you need the glasses with amber lights in the bedroom. It looks exactly the same to me. ### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-07T14:05:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/glasses-for-nightime.6104/post-79451>

LASIK is an operation where you don't want to base a decision on price. Sure some place advertise LASIK at $ 250 .... but they are production lines, and don't forget the fine print "starting at".

Prior to having my LASIK procedure, I carefully researched the best doctor. I distinctly remember sitting in the waiting room of the doctor I selected and hearing other patients talk about coming to this doctor for a "touchup" of their previous LASIK.

Also, a good LASIK doc will reject prospective patients that don't meet the key criteria .... poor LASIK candidates can include; people with dry eyes, a cornea that is too thin, and astigmatism out of specified range.

Sure contacts and/or glasses are a pain .... but a botched LASIK would be much worse. Best advice, do your homework.

>

iPads are evil! LOL I feel that way about _any_ of the newer devices that can only get on the internet with wi-fi. How could they have designed them like that? Imagine how many people are creating their own wi-fi hotspots all over the place just to be able to get on their iPads and smartphones? They can (and do!) create wi-fi from a wired access point with a portable router like [this one](http://www.amazon.com/TP-LINK-TL-WR702N-Wireless-Repeater-150Mpbs/dp/B007PTCFFW). Wi-fi: it's everywhere and it's inescapable.

A grounded laptop or a desktop, hardwired into the internet (and with a wired external mouse and keyboard) is much safer. Of course, you still need f.lux and amber glasses to reduce the blue light coming from the screen.

I could not help to comment on the vitamin D webinar, I donâ€™t take any vitamin D and I go to be about 8.0pm after been working on the computer in the dark, my husband bought an orange filter to put on the screen so I donâ€™t need to wear any orange glasses.

R 250H Vitamin D

Just reread the Vitamin D blog, and found this nugget by Jack in the comments. [http://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/](http://www.jackkruse.com/the-sunshine-of-your-life/)

In response the radiation questions I have gotten by email here goes. As a spine surgeon I use Xray daily in the OR. We also have badges that track our radiation exposure. I keep mine in my pocket at waist level because this is where I get the most exposure. Since I have optimized my vitamin D level my radiation levels have dropped by 120% over the last 5 yrs. For completeness sake I also take some supllemental Iodine twice a week and that will help too. The point being that an optimal vitamin D level will protect your from Xrays and from UV radiation. And I am very fair skinned and freckled and have always burned easy. I still burn here and there but I have not used sunscreen at all in 5 yrs and I am constantly sun exposed now. I used to worry about the sun a lot. I don't fret over it any longer. I would if I ate bread wheat and a SAD however.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-01T05:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78456>

From the new series.............Quantum biology Have you ever put a bottle of water in the freezer before bed and awaken in the AM to take it out and drink and it begins to freeze top to bottom in seconds? Well if you have then it means your water is super clean and has inherent abilities to become supercooled without freezing even at -20 C? Water needs something to start it to crystalize......some impurity, dust, kink in its container to begin the nucleation or crystallization of ice to begin. This is an example of water memory. When water is super pure it is considered metastable in its liquid state making water act strange to some who have never seen it. This is how I do deep CT in water below 30 degrees without frostbite. This strangeness is what allows super conduction to occur, because in a cooled superconductor the current speeds up and not down as it does in an ionic fluid. This is more common in desalinated or RO water because it is so pure............

### IceBarbie — 2013-03-01T05:25:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78458>

We had a RO filter and our ice cubes always had these cool inverted icicles sticking up from the cubes--some have been 2" tall by 1/4" wide. Then our filters needed replacing and we didn't get around to it. But now we noticed that our well water causes the same thing from time to time. I'm hoping that's a good thing. I've never noticed these icicles on the cubes anywhere else I've lived.

### AK Man — 2013-03-01T07:24:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78471>

I would think the phenomenon of bottled water freezing upon opening would more likely be due to the pressure gradient. Water under pressure can become supercooled and opening the bottle relieves the pressure and allows freezing.

An even more interesting water fact comes from plants. Have you ever noticed the USDA Plant Hardiness Zone maps? The zone indicates the coldest expected temperature in those areas. Plants hardy down to 32 deg F will quickly die if the temp hits 25 deg F. There are plants that can survive down to -60 (Zone 1). This is possible because plants that evolved at the temps have the ability to super-purify the water in their cells, tropical plants obviously don't have this ability, but arctic plants do. Water can actually remain in a fluid state down to its homogeneous nucleation point of approximately 231 K (−43 deg F).

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-78473>

water...........facts.......memory of water.

I prefer to think of this case as energy imprinting rather than the memory of water. (Of course the effect is equivalent.) The substance leaves its energy signature imprinted in the water, & even after it is gone, the water can perform as its proxy. And everything has a discrete energy signature.

Two hydrogen atoms meet. One says, â€˜Iâ€™ve lost my electron.â€™ The other says â€˜Are you sure?â€™ The first replies, â€˜Yes, Iâ€™m positive.â€™

In order to lower the freezing point of water 1 C. requires a pressure of 135 atm, subtle, but there.

Most triple-phase charts show it as this:

Pressure (atmospheres)/Freezing Point, in degC
1/ 0
1,000/ -10
2,045 / -22
3,420 / -17
6,160 / 0

### bogman — 2013-03-17T18:49:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-80870>

.
I hope this helps to clarify how important elecrical charge is in the body. Even in college level chemistry classes they don't mention pH is tied to electrical potential in our body. More importantly how the change in pH alters electrical potential causing disease or healing to take place. I could be wrong here so feel free to correct something I missed

Crazy Water company makes a **No. 4 mineral water** that is the strongest mineral water on the market. More than 2,600 TDS and a proper pH level. A pH of 7.00 has a zero electrical charge - only with a pH lower @ 7.00 can water conduct electricity. 7.1 to 14.9 ==== Distilled water is 7.00 which means it can NOT carry electrical charge.

The electrical potential is measured in water with an ORP meter which measures the very slight voltage in the H2O. When we do this we are actually measuring the presence of oxidizing or reducing agents by their specific electrical charge, thus Oxidation Reduction Potential. High pH (alkaline) water has more "reducing" agents (negative \[-\]ORP) and low pH (acidic) water has more oxidizing agents (positive \[+\]ORP). measured in Millivolts (mv)

An oxidizing agent, it is expressed in +ORP \\ this steals electrons causing free radical which is not helpful to healing.

A reducing agent is expressed in terms of â€“ORP \\ this is termed an "anti-oxident" that counteracts free radicals, good for healing.

Therefore, acidic foods and drinks have a pH above 7.00 / ranging from 0.00 to 7.00 which steals your electrons causing "DIS" ease.
The human body functions best with a pH hovering around 7.35 to 7.45 or in electrical terms, -20 mV to -40 mV. Some teenagers can hover between -40 mV to -60 mV they are bouncing all over the place with energy. If your pH dips below optimal that means your electrical charge drops below healthy levels - Cancer can only develop in a certain pH range & therefore, your electrical charge will drop below -5 mV all the way to +50 mV. In order to heal from disease/injury you need to become more basic with your pH to bring the electrical charge back to healthly levels (-20 mV to -40 mV) That means you need to be over -60 mV before you can heal. Without getting to in depth.... Cancer takes 1 mol of glucose to make only 2 ATP - a healthy cell takes 1 mol of glucose to make 38 ATP. That means the Cancer Cell needs to make 19 x's more Glucose to survive. It's in panic mode looking for any extra Glucose. How does it fix this problem? Cancer Cells figured out how to produce insulin receptors - sort of akin to fly paper to attract more flies (glucose). Pretty clever. That's a whole other topic that gets deep fast.

The real kicker is EMF, stress, life ect... all tend to steal your electrons causing your pH to be altered which leads to a loss in your electrical potential from healthy -20 to -40 / to unhealthy levels or +ORP. A tiny change in pH from a healthy level of 7.65 & your health tanks causing your pH to fall to 7.35, sounds like a small charge right? However, pH is measured in a Logarithmic Scale. Therefore, 7.35 is 1,000 times more acidic than 7.65, a much larger change than we realize.

This is way you can drink tap water til your bladder will explode and you're still dehydrated.......... drum roll....

The ORP of most tap water in the USA is between +200 to +600mv and so is an oxidizing agent !!!

So much for regular water being healthy - furthermore, bottled fancy pants water is worse. Most bottled water is over +500 mV!
If you're on the treadmill running your buns off and drinking bottled water it's like riding a bike & smoking at the same time.

Drinking mineral water from Mineral Wells, TX - aka Crazy Water - will help keep you hydrated & in proper pH range. Mineral water will help your body to conduct it's electrical current. Equals healthy.

Why do Vitamin C IV's help ? Ask this question to a Naturopath or Medical Doctor sometime. They won't know the answer!
The answer is: you're giving electrons. Electrons = healthy you. A healthy cell with good electrical charge burns ATP better. Your engine is running like a race car & not like a lawn mower engine (unhealthy). Did you know a fever is the body's amazing ability to turn up the furnance to create more energy (more electricity) It's the body saying help! & going into healing mode. Cold blooded animals, a lizard, when they get injuried seek a warm place (laying in the sun) to increase their body's temp - therefore, increasing their healing ability. What happens now when you get sick? DRUGS ! Masks the body's own ability to heal. You only get worse.
Try explaining that to your family doctor - they'll think you need to be in a padded room lol

Before the Great Flood during Noah's time on Earth - people did in fact live over the age of 200. Can't be done? The men of Monte Picchu are fathering children at 110 years old! No Viagra - they can get a healthy erection at 110 years old  WOW right! Our ultra-modern lifestyle of today is killing us. Time to go back to living in nature, naked, free from modern life.

Cooked food causes the body to produce more white blood cells. The body sees food as an evader causing our pH to get worse. Even pain causes the body to freak out and cause a loss of electrons. Why does lidocaine help? yep! Gives you more electrons and therefore, less pain.

Anywho - hope helps. I know there's so much to understand and learn.

Voltage = pH

one last point. Did you know you can take a voltmeter & measure the mV of your teeth? if a tooth is dead or has a metal filling it'll show up as +ORP. A dentist uses this same device (called Cavitat or Oral Potential Meter) the dentist can see early decay in your tooth. Who knew.... Sad that dental schools don't teach the importance of this concept - only thing they teach is reading x is bad and reading y is good. ugh

Can you tell I love to dig deep into electrical potential & pH

If your pH dips below optimal that means your electrical charge drops below healthy levels - Cancer can only develop in a certain pH range & therefore, your electrical charge will drop below -5 mV all the way to +50 mV. In order to heal from disease/injury you need to become more basic with your pH to bring the electrical charge back to healthly levels (-20 mV to -40 mV) That means you need to be over -60 mV before you can heal. Without getting to in depth.... Cancer takes 1 mol of glucose to make only 2 ATP - a healthy cell takes 1 mol of glucose to make 38 ATP. That means the Cancer Cell needs to make 19 x's more Glucose to survive. It's in panic mode looking for any extra Glucose. How does it fix this problem? Cancer Cells figured out how to produce insulin receptors - sort of akin to fly paper to attract more flies (glucose). Pretty clever. That's a whole other topic that gets deep fast.

The real kicker is EMF, stress, life ect... all tend to steal your electrons causing your pH to be altered which leads to a loss in your electrical potential from healthy -20 to -40 / to unhealthy levels or +ORP. A tiny change in pH from a healthy level of 7.65 & your health tanks causing your pH to fall to 7.35, sounds like a small charge right? However, pH is measured in a Logarithmic Scale. Therefore, 7.35 is 1,000 times more acidic than 7.65, a much larger change than we realize.

This is way you can drink tap water til your bladder will explode and you're still dehydrated.......... drum roll....

The ORP of most tap water in the USA is between +200 to +600mv and so is an oxidizing agent !!!

So much for regular water being healthy - furthermore, bottled fancy pants water is worse. Most bottled water is over +500 mV!
If you're on the treadmill running your buns off and drinking bottled water it's like riding a bike & smoking at the same time.

Drinking mineral water from Mineral Wells, TX - aka Crazy Water - will help keep you hydrated & in proper pH range. Mineral water will help your body to conduct it's electrical current. Equals healthy.

Why do Vitamin C IV's help ? Ask this question to a Naturopath or Medical Doctor sometime. They won't know the answer!
The answer is: you're giving electrons. Electrons = healthy you. A healthy cell with good electrical charge burns ATP better. Your engine is running like a race car & not like a lawn mower engine (unhealthy). Did you know a fever is the body's amazing ability to turn up the furnance to create more energy (more electricity) It's the body saying help! & going into healing mode. Cold blooded animals, a lizard, when they get injuried seek a warm place (laying in the sun) to increase their body's temp - therefore, increasing their healing ability. What happens now when you get sick? DRUGS ! Masks the body's own ability to heal. You only get worse.
Try explaining that to your family doctor - they'll think you need to be in a padded room lol

Before the Great Flood during Noah's time on Earth - people did in fact live over the age of 200. Can't be done? The men of Monte Picchu are fathering children at 110 years old! No Viagra - they can get a healthy erection at 110 years old  WOW right! Our ultra-modern lifestyle of today is killing us. Time to go back to living in nature, naked, free from modern life.

Cooked food causes the body to produce more white blood cells. The body sees food as an evader causing our pH to get worse. Even pain causes the body to freak out and cause a loss of electrons. Why does lidocaine help? yep! Gives you more electrons and therefore, less pain.

Anywho - hope helps. I know there's so much to understand and learn.

Voltage = pH

one last point. Did you know you can take a voltmeter & measure the mV of your teeth? if a tooth is dead or has a metal filling it'll show up as +ORP. A dentist uses this same device (called Cavitat or Oral Potential Meter) the dentist can see early decay in your tooth. Who knew.... Sad that dental schools don't teach the importance of this concept - only thing they teach is reading x is bad and reading y is good. ugh

Can you tell I love to dig deep into electrical potential & pH

### bonsaiz — 2013-05-13T11:14:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/water-facts.6122/post-85937>

> [AK Man said:](/goto/post?id=78471)
>
> I would think the phenomenon of bottled water freezing upon opening would more likely be due to the pressure gradient. Water under pressure can become supercooled and opening the bottle relieves the pressure and allows freezing.
>
> An even more interesting water fact comes from plants. Have you ever noticed the USDA Plant Hardiness Zone maps? The zone indicates the coldest expected temperature in those areas. Plants hardy down to 32 deg F will quickly die if the temp hits 25 deg F. There are plants that can survive down to -60 (Zone 1). This is possible because plants that evolved at the temps have the ability to super-purify the water in their cells, tropical plants obviously don't have this ability, but arctic plants do. Water can actually remain in a fluid state down to its homogeneous nucleation point of approximately 231 K (−43 deg F).

Coincidence. I thought the same about the water bottle thing. But as much as you are describing.
