### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-05T03:13:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-labs-back-input-hack-needed-please.6167/post-78995>

Time to step your game up......look at your local environments.......EMF and fake light are the biggies.......then you go after water and then hormones.

### Hope — 2013-03-06T03:33:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-labs-back-input-hack-needed-please.6167/post-79248>

And when you're done with the EMF/artificial light, maybe check out [http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-12-dare-to-disagree/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-12-dare-to-disagree/)

and plug in your Ft3/RT3 into here: [http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/](http://www.stopthethyroidmadness.com/rt3-ratio/) you want that to be 20 or higher but looks like with your super low (is it really 2.06 or 2.6??) FT3 yours will be less than 20.

I would take the kelp no matter what...seaweeds are great...you can put them in water or broths/soup etc. You really need iodine....it looks like. If you don't have enough, you can't make enough of any thyroid hormones. I would try to up your iodine/kelp/seaweed intake...most people are deficient. I was extremely low and getting more sufficient has really helped (I also have hypothyroidism). The mitochondrial Rx would be good for you....though you need the dpi-paleo Rx as well.....

From BG 12:
The larger the brain the mammal has the more iodine levels in the dietary nutrients becomes critical. IE: The brains evolutionary construction is what controls the decision process of what foods we should be determining contains optimal nutrient density, not someone’s belief of such. Iodine is not conserved in human physiology to any great degree. This implies that our environment always had a deep supply, from an evolutionary standpoint. This foundational fact, is in many people’s blind spot today. It is underscored when you consider that human physiology has no source to concentrate or reserve iodine for future use at all in human physiology. A constant source is required for proper neurologic and thyroid functioning. I want you to examine it and realize why iodine is critical when you have a massive brain.

Here is a great blog on the different seaweeds:

[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-6-epi-paleo-rx/)

So much to read/learn/change/do....

From a fellow new learner.....good luck!

### Justfourme — 2013-03-07T05:04:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/first-labs-back-input-hack-needed-please.6167/post-79428>

Yes, my free t3 results were really 2.06 (reference range 2.50 to 4.30)

TSH was 1.5 (range 0.27 to 4.20)
RT3 was15.8 (range 9.2 to 24.1)

Doc agrees that my free t3 is super low. Working on that, taking my iodine/seaweeds, mega dose vit D3, eating fish.
My cortisol and adrenal tests will be back in a week or so. Expecting some low numbers there too and looking forward to starting BHRT. (53yo female nearing menopause)

Will look at testing EMF - would not be surprised if very high at work as this new job I took a year ago has resulted in several areas of declining health and weight gain. I feel so much better even when I work at home a couple days away from that building.

# Thread: My N=1:  Earthing for sleep apnea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Optimalbound — 2013-03-04T21:33:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-78946>

I was unaware that my sleep apnea might be caused by autoimmunity until Jack mentioned it a while ago in Ask Jack. Then, when Jack recently recommended earthing as especially helpful for those with autoimmune disease, I immediately knew I just had to do my own earthing N=1.

So I've been walking on the beach nearly every day for the past ten days. Bare feet and wading or walking where the sand is wet from the waves. The sea is barely above freezing here these days, so in the beginning I only lasted 5 minutes, but to my surprise I was quickly up to 25 minutes without much problems. Feels great.

Lo and behold, I have now had several nights of sleeping well without my CPAP machine! This is a big win for me. I guess it's too early to tell causation from correlation with absolute certainty, and I need further progress, but my N=1 so far certainly feels wildly promising!

Thanks, Jack, for leaving Ask Jack open and readable even for us lowly bronze members. Otherwise, I would probably have missed out on these little gems of information regarding autoimmunity.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-04T22:38:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-78960>

Getting off the CPAP machine is a biggie. Glad you are seeing improvement so quickly!

### diane — 2013-03-04T22:49:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-78964>

Optimalbound - that is huge! Thanks for sharing your experience so that others can benefit as well.

### Optimalbound — 2013-03-04T23:03:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-78965>

Thanks, Nonchalant and Diane.

One thing that concerns me, is that I experienced a similar improvement last summer when I swam in the ocean regularly. At the time I thought it was the CT that did it, but the effect waned gradually when fall came and my CTing was in the cold tub instead of the ocean. I'm guessing that this reversal is an indication that there is something in my environment that I need to address... Which reminds me to schedule a consult with Dr.K soon. I've been procrastinating for way too long.

### Optimalbound — 2013-03-06T00:14:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-79197>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=79038)
>
> Optimalbound,
> thank you for sharing...! Wow.
> Have you been picking oysters at the coast of Norway? My sister lives in Stavanger and she was at the ocean the other day, she wrote me an email and told me there was so many oysters there, she could see plenty...! She is still too scared to eat them but I hope she will soon do. I need to go there this summer and teach her how to do! OMG I cannot believe what treasures you have.. so much oysters you can eat all for free and totally fresh!

Hi Inger,

Yes, I've picked a few oysters, and even more scallops and blue mussels. But not as often as I'd like, so I aim to do more of it this year. I'm not sure how scallops and blue mussels compare with oysters on the epi-paleo scale of goodness, by the way.. Oysters and scallops are delicious raw, but I just haven't managed to convince myself to try that with the blue mussels, yet. They're lovely steamed in whtite wine and garlic, though. I've caught some crayfish as well, but the traps need to be set quite deep; 300-400 feet, so it is quite a job to pull them up again!

### Jonathan108 — 2014-03-06T12:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-113749>

Walking on the beach is a great way to do earthing, but most people don't live near a beach. Most don't even have a patch of grass where they can safely walk barefoot. So, impoving sleep by earthing requires a device which you can get from [earthing.com](http://www.earthing.com/Default.asp). This allows you to stay grounded all night long. For the do-it-yourselfer (or cheapskate like me), it's easy to make a simple effective device. I've been using [this one](http://www.naturesplatform.com/earthing.html) for the past four years, with great success.

### helios_brah — 2024-04-09T22:06:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-333454>

> [Optimalbound said:](/goto/post?id=78946)
>
> I was unaware that my sleep apnea might be caused by autoimmunity until Jack mentioned it a while ago in Ask Jack. Then, when Jack recently recommended earthing as especially helpful for those with autoimmune disease, I immediately knew I just had to do my own earthing N=1.
>
> So I've been walking on the beach nearly every day for the past ten days. Bare feet and wading or walking where the sand is wet from the waves. The sea is barely above freezing here these days, so in the beginning I only lasted 5 minutes, but to my surprise I was quickly up to 25 minutes without much problems. Feels great.
>
> Lo and behold, I have now had several nights of sleeping well without my CPAP machine! This is a big win for me. I guess it's too early to tell causation from correlation with absolute certainty, and I need further progress, but my N=1 so far certainly feels wildly promising!
>
> Thanks, Jack, for leaving Ask Jack open and readable even for us lowly bronze members. Otherwise, I would probably have missed out on these little gems of information regarding autoimmunity.

Did you resolve your sleep apnea with that protocol in the end?

### Optimalbound — 2024-04-10T16:42:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-n-1-earthing-for-sleep-apnea.6168/post-333474>

Yeah, but I find that if I am stop earthing regularly, my sleep apnea slowly comes back again.

### Raiken3712 — 2013-03-04T22:16:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/extra-skin-issues-from-weight-loss.6170/post-78955>

I wanted to post some good information in a calorie count thread because I know these issues can be hard on people. I would prefer not to have a bunch of extra skin when I finally get around to ending the binging and actually losing most of my weight. So for many and also so I can relate this to others I'm curious about how people here have dealt with this.

Does eating Epi-Paleo and using coconut oil on the skin help? Are there other things people have done that were effective without the use of surgery? Does CT help the skin? Many people are probably not going to even think of trying something like CT, but I'm curious if its helped anyone with skin issues.

[http://caloriecount.about.com/forums/motivation/feel-better-weight-loss](http://caloriecount.about.com/forums/motivation/feel-better-weight-loss)

### caroline — 2013-04-02T03:09:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-where-you-live-matter-3-4-13-post.6173/post-82549>

I think maybe this is a sixth sense thing ...... where do you innately feel better? where do you long to be if you had the choice/chance? This isn't an overnite decision.....afterall it took JK three years to figure it out and he had a lot more information and tools at his disposal. Don't forget that Dr. K. is about 5-6 years ahead of us.

I really do think we know when something feels right - maybe that is over simplifying - and then you got to look at good, better and best situations for you and your family. We need to weigh up all the positives and negatives and figure out how to implement the changes we need to make. It also really depends on your health...as JK has said ...his day job is over the top awash in EMF's etc. and if he wants to live a long,healthy, productive life he had to do something pretty radical and I think some others here will as well.

I think BJ - here on the forum - is a perfect example. At first she was overwhelmed and scared and wanted to move immediately.... and now she has spent time and done some due diligence and checked her environment and weighed up all the negatives and positives and understands that maybe with a few changes - they will be okay where they are. So many things come into play for each of us ....and our happiness does have to figure into the decision too.

I think we all need to reflect on our happiness quotient .....the healthiest place for us might - in the long run - makes us miserable. so - Maybe second best??

There is so much to consider and it is not easy - but it will and should take time. We need to become centred and meditate and things will become clearer....

And we are getting more tools .....with the EMF expert.....

### Jack Kruse — 2020-04-04T17:35:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-where-you-live-matter-3-4-13-post.6173/post-288345>

Living in NYC during C-19 was not a wise choice for millions.........when will they wake up and become Black Swans?
I call modern extinction events "Factor Y". We have had 5 previous extinctions. Modern man has learned nothing about any of them otherwise they'd realize some of the things we have co-opted to communicate are harming us during this pandemic.

What does the inside of the sixth extinction look like to a human? Here is that picture painted in words. A pandemic is like a wave or a surge of water. A surge is, by definition, time-limited. What happens when doctors can’t see the end of the tsunami? Despair sets in for them. Health is never more than one generation away from extinction. Few of us realize it. I teach my tribe to see the truth before it bites them in the ass. Humans forget how the rules of life are written by Nature. Extinction is the rule. Survival is the exception.

Though it might be nice to imagine there once was a time when man lived in harmony with nature, it’s not clear that he ever really did when you are a student of history.

We're so self-important. So arrogant of how our aberrant use of the electromagnetic spectrum fueled this virus. We're no different than the dinosaurs. Another failed mutation; another closed-end biological mistake. We just cannot fathom how our choices played the riptide in this pandemic that created the waves that are surging in our hospitals now.

Mankind's Dunning Kruger effect is thus: **IN PUSHING OTHER SPECIES TO EXTINCTION WITH ITS ABERRANT USE OF THE** _**electromagnetic spectrum,**_ **HUMANITY IS BUSY SAWING OFF THE LIMB ON WHICH IT PERCHES.**

That is what I see when I read this link below.

[https://www.newyorker.com/science/medical-dispatch/new-yorks-hospitals-take-on-the-coronavirus](https://www.newyorker.com/science/medical-dispatch/new-yorks-hospitals-take-on-the-coronavirus)

# Thread: NYT article - Negative reader comments can distort what readers think was reported
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-05T17:00:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79099>

[http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/03/opinion/sunday/this-story-stinks.html?\_r=0](http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/03/opinion/sunday/this-story-stinks.html?_r=0)

The Web, it should be said, is still a marvelous place for public debate. But when it comes to reading and understanding news stories online â€” like this one, for example â€” the medium can have a surprisingly potent effect on the message. Comments from some readers, our research shows, can significantly distort what other readers think was reported in the first place.

But here, itâ€™s not the content of the comments that matters. Itâ€™s the tone.

\----------------

Simply including **an ad hominem attack in a reader comment was enough to make study participants think the downside of the reported technology was greater than theyâ€™d previously thought.**

While itâ€™s hard to quantify the distortional effects of such online nastiness, itâ€™s bound to be quite substantial, particularly â€” and perhaps ironically â€” in the area of science news.

An estimated 60 percent of the Americans seeking information about specific scientific matters say the Internet is their primary source of information â€” ranking it higher than any other news source.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-05T17:02:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79101>

This is why I laugh when I hear docs say no one gets their info from the internet......my perception is 95% do now.

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-05T17:12:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79104>

and the other 5% get their info from TV ads for food and drugs

### nonchalant — 2013-03-05T17:37:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79106>

> [ATL\_Paleo said:](/goto/post?id=79099)
>
> [http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/03/opinion/sunday/this-story-stinks.html?\_r=0](http://www.nytimes.com/2013/03/03/opinion/sunday/this-story-stinks.html?_r=0)
>
> Simply including **an ad hominem attack in a reader comment was enough to make study participants think the downside of the reported technology was greater than they’d previously thought.**

That explains it. The out-of-proportion reply to some new information. I would always wonder why people would give such emotional, inflammatory responses. It definitely seems to work in politics, also.

### kathylu — 2013-03-05T18:33:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79120>

That must be why I was completely ignoring the recent dust-up here. I don't pay attention to the news anymore...

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-05T18:37:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79122>

me neither......it is a waste of good neurons.

### diane — 2013-03-05T20:43:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79160>

I have to ignore comments on articles. I've read through them a few times and it just dashed my hopes for humanity. It's way too depressing to read them.

### DarleenMB — 2013-03-05T21:27:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79176>

I gave up on the news 25 years ago. My attitude has been that if something earth shaking occurs SOMEbody will call me and tell me about it.

I also discovered the nasty effect all on my own quite a while back (which I dubbed the "torches and pitchforks" effect). Which is why, when I see the beginnings of trollish behavior, I run for the hills and don't look back.

### caroline — 2013-03-05T23:40:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nyt-article-negative-reader-comments-can-distort-what-readers-think-was-reported.6183/post-79193>

>
> Well, one (small) city does - but it has to start somewhere. Hopefully the government in Toronto will wake up! But highly doubtful....

Very doubtful, since too much of the water supply comes from Toronto....unless of course someone educates the public and it gets put to a referendum....That is why Windsor and similar communities were able to do it, they don't depend on T.O. T.O. hasn't pronounced itself, it is sitting on the back burner still. But if a City as big as Toronto gets the fluoride out, it will be a domino effect, which is what I think the powers that be are afraid of. It's all PR....what would people think if all of a sudden fluoride was banned as a health issue when they have allowed it for the past 60 years , denying its deleterious effects? It incriminates the gov't, the dentists, the ministry of environment, etc...they all lose credibility? So now, people begin to question - everything? Can't have that...! because god forbid, they may question something else as well. If you read the comments section to the above link , you will see that some think that fluoride is needed for bacteria control??????? OMG, seriously, today there is no excuse for people being misinformed on any subject, **if they want to know.**

# Thread: CT use in CF patients?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-use-in-cf-patients.6193/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-03-05T21:24:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-use-in-cf-patients.6193/post-79174>

My girlfriend is in her mid 20's and has Cystic Fibrosis: [http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystic\_fibrosis](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cystic_fibrosis). She is looking to try some new strategies to improve her health, and CT is one she is considering. I agree that it could be very beneficial, but I think the protocol may need modifications to assure it works properly considering her condition...

My concern is, I have not found any documentation of other cystic fibrosis patients using CT in their treatment regimen. Does anyone know of existing cases, and whether it was a positive or negative experience? Or, possibly other cases of individuals with other dibilitating lung disorders? Please share if so!

Since cystic fibrosis impacts the lungs so significantly (her lung function equates to roughly 50% of "normal" for her stats), my guess would be her ramp-up time (working from face dips -> ice packs -> bath) would be extended, and also her exposure time reduced. Obviously close temp monitoring would be used, but should she aim for the same minimum skin temps, or add an extra margin of error?

She is going to discuss it with her Dr's as well, but considering the medical community prefers the Rx bandaid, I fear that her docs will turn down the idea due to a lack of knowledge.

Any thoughts/comments pertaining to this experiment are appreciated, thanks for reading!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-05T22:23:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-use-in-cf-patients.6193/post-79183>

CT is huge for cystic fibrosis........especially in the ocean.

# Thread: Aikido and CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aikido-and-ct.6195/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Dali Dula — 2013-03-06T01:42:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aikido-and-ct.6195/post-79209>

I have practiced Aikido for over twenty years. Maybe this is why I found CT so accessible. check out this post I found from 2004 on a aikido forum.

[http://www.aikiweb.com/forums/showthread.php?t=6223](http://www.aikiweb.com/forums/showthread.php?t=6223)

William Reed wrote:
From "Ki : A Road Anyone can Travel" pg 211...Drawn from Shinto rituals of purification, Sen Shin no Gyo (Training to Cleanse the Mind) involves dousing the body with several buckets of cold water first thing on arising. This is done year round, even in the dead of winter...pg 247...Ueshiba (the founder) rose every morning at 4:00 A.M., doused his body with cold water, and then went outside to greet the morning sun, meditating before the Aiki Shrine for an hour and a half, feeling the communion of being in harmony with the universe...He would practice chinkon-kishin, a Shinto meditation technique for centering the mind and body, with gestures and invocations performed at sacred spots and shrines. He also practiced misogi, the ritual washing away of impurities with cold water, in streams and waterfalls.

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-06T01:50:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aikido-and-ct.6195/post-79211>

sounds like a smart dude..........he must know a bit of something about how circadian cycles work huh? See this is why intuition is big........several people on this forum are pissed at the folks here who are intuitive.......well the ancients knew a lot without having an education in science.......observations about us matter. It is time everyone realize intuition is the sixth sense when our brain is maximally functioning. This is Brain gut 14 all over again.......[http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-14-personal-ground-zero/](http://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-14-personal-ground-zero/)

### Chrysanthemama — 2013-03-06T18:55:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/aikido-and-ct.6195/post-79310>

I took Aikido for a couple years and misogi was something my husband and I always enjoyed. We were living in the Pacific NW at the time, and misogi was held in the cold waters of the Puget Sound. How interesting about Ueshiba! That sounds like a wonderful way to start each day.

I didn't even like much seafood before the Epi-paleo RX. I still am not an oyster lover, but I love how they make me feel. I couldn't have be happier when i gave up the oil for lots of seafood.

### caroline — 2013-03-06T21:16:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79325>

I have always loved fish but - man - am I having a problem with oysters now....and that is the one thing I should be eating by the bucket load ....

### freesia — 2013-03-06T23:34:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79355>

Tis Bluff oyster season here now Caroline. I've had my first raw ones ever this week.  C'mon you can do it!

I just used lemon juice, a teeny bit of seasalt and black pepper. I had some initial "hey-what-raw?-no-this-can't-be-right!" nonsense going on my head but once the first one was eaten (which was delicious!) I found they're kind of addictive. Though they're a bit pricy here!
I was surprised from Matt Lalondes talk at AHS13 that using his criteria clams rated so highly, when compared to scallops.
...Fresh clams are cheap as chips here.

### kathylu — 2013-03-07T04:06:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79420>

Keep in mind they are talking about the ethyl ester of omega 3 here. That's the active ingredient of Lovaza, a prescription fish oil. In order to patent it, they had to do a chemical modification not found in nature...thus the esterified version. Not the same at all as fish oil simply extracted from fishies...just sayin'. Sort of like a progestin instead of progesterone....

### freesia — 2013-03-07T08:04:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79433>

Thanks Kathy
Chemical modifications I can do without I think - unless they're cleverly organised endogenous ones...

### caroline — 2013-03-07T10:12:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79437>

> [freesia said:](/goto/post?id=79355)
>
> Tis Bluff oyster season here now Caroline. I've had my first raw ones ever this week.  C'mon you can do it!
>
> I just used lemon juice, a teeny bit of seasalt and black pepper. I had some initial "hey-what-raw?-no-this-can't-be-right!" nonsense going on my head but once the first one was eaten (which was delicious!) I found they're kind of addictive. Though they're a bit pricy here!
> I was surprised from Matt Lalondes talk at AHS13 that using his criteria clams rated so highly, when compared to scallops.
> ...Fresh clams are cheap as chips here.

The thing is Anne - I have been eating oysters for a year and didn't mind them at all but I got sick the last two times and now struggle really badly.
I had three this morning and three tonite - but not happy. I had a swig of wine right out of the bottle - couldn't wait to find a glass!! and then 2 dill pickles!!
what a gourmet combination do ya think???

Honestly I would much rather have a MHS .... i bet it would be a good idea to throw some oysters in that.... gonna think about that.....

So - clams are almost as good? I have been thinking of making a clam chowder. Are Shrimp almost as good too? I seem to be eating bucket loads lately.

### kathylu — 2013-03-07T17:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79471>

I much prefer clams to oysters. Someone needs to do a nutritional comparison...maybe clams are just as good? I hope!

### HelenaVon — 2013-03-07T17:34:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79477>

What about DHA from a seaweed source?

AND Are canned oysters okay to eat? I saw most are farmed from China( kind of a scary thought)
I think I might be able to stomach clams better but are you guys talking canned clams / fresh raw or cooked ones?

### janagram — 2013-03-08T18:22:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79674>

I have only been able to eat scallops so far....but long ago I loved pasta with clam sauce....hellllp! Let's find out which are best!

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-08T21:29:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79696>

> [HelenaVon said:](/goto/post?id=79477)
>
> Are canned oysters okay to eat? I saw most are farmed from China( kind of a scary thought)

Canned oysters are a great sub for the fresh. Also a lot of them still come from Korea too. Not sure that's any better but as Dr. K says the worst fish is better than grass-fed organ meat. I love the Crown Prince Naturally Smoked Oysters and Napoleon Tiny Smoked Oysters.

### freesia — 2013-03-08T21:57:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ive-always-said-eat-fish-and-not-fish-oil.6205/post-79701>

Most local fresh shellfish here seems to have seasonal availability, so that's how and when I've been eating them. I love scallops but they were not rated as highly as clams according to Matt Lalonde's criteria at AHS12.
(You can find his talk on youtube.)

My mother said she got cravings for oysters when she was pregnant with me! They were just out of season so my dad brought home canned ones. Not sure where they were from, and this was a long while ago (!) but they had added msg and gave her a horrible migraine. I'd hope canned shellfish now wouldn't have nasty additives....just the good stuff and wash-offable oil hopefully?

New to all of this and new to forums! Starting a journal to keep me accountable. Please feel free to provide (much needed) feedback, I love to learn!

Attempting the 90 day optimal reset!
5'6
58kgs
32 years old
2 lovely twin baby girls
1 fab husband!
Love to be inspired and hope to inspire!
No labs yet- anyone who can assist with directing me to the most basic level of labs would be great!

Goals- to love life, achieve self love and self acceptance.
To sleep better, be calmer, more energy and perform in the gym like a badass.

I start now!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-07T14:07:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79452>

I like new journals. Why? It causes you to reflect. Most of us have stopped doing it now because we have technology loading our heads with other peoples ideas........Every morning I post my best thought on FB and today I decided to put it here.

Have you lived half a life to this point? It is time to embrace the small area under the curve of life called the positive 3rd standard deviation. Who do you think you are ? Exceptional should be your only option. To live exceptionally in tail of life you have to have the gut to do what only 5% are willing to do to get it. If your life is severed short are you ready for that consequence? Are you even aware of this possibility? Are you willing to take the risk to empty all your pockets full of miracles to leave here on empty? Well forgive yourself for your faults and your mistakes and move on. Don't make excuses for why you cant get it done. Focus on all the reasons why you must make it happen. Never be limited by other people's limited imaginations. Today go get it.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=79553)
>
> That is what the words ....."when you know better...you do better" means to me. Forgive yourself for your faults and shortcomings and mistakes and move on.
>
> I love what you said "take the risk to empty your pocket full of miracles to leave here on empty" That is my new motto!! thanks...so little time - so much to do.....
>
> I hope you are still going to post your thoughts on FB?

Thanks Jack and Caroline! I have absolutely been living half a life, based on others expectations! I know I can live better, be better and feel better and I have been scared to do it by myself, wanting really to just belong, therefore not living how I've needed to for most of my life!
Now I have decided that its really ok to not always fit in, it's more important to me now ( motherhood has done this) to be inspired and to inspire, so that's what my aim is to do, alone, or otherwise. Wish me luck!

So I'm going to break this journal up into 5 parts- song of the day, vibe (my thoughts), food, training, and sleep. This is to primarily assist me in looking at the many facets of this reset, to stop me from just focussing on just one or two.

Song for the day- "Anyway you want it"
[http://m.youtube.com/#/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&desktop\_uri=/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&gl=GB](http://m.youtube.com/#/watch?v=atxUuldUcfI&desktop_uri=%2Fwatch%3Fv%3DatxUuldUcfI&gl=GB)
\- try to watch this without smiling or grooving just a little bit!

Sleep- less than awesome, my girls had the most horrendous bout of gastro, which saw us into the emrgancey section of our hospital 3 times! The best effort this week was a broken 7 hours and that was after 5 days of not much sleep a all!
Girls are much better now, thank goodness so we'll see.

Training- weight training 4 times this week with some HIIT and sprints. Cold showers every day for about 10 minutes. The C.T thing is so new to me so I thought cold showers may be a great place to start? I have felt exhausted most of this week so my training didn't have much energy, but I showed up and put on some tunes and I was good to go!

Food- eggs, seafood, nori, greens, avocado, prawns, scallops, tuna and salmon, oh and bacon and coconut oil. I am NOT used to eating like this but I am LOVIN it and trying so hard to not to count these calories in my head! I am a reformed (because I said so! ) binge eater/excersiser, low fat, fruit eating vegetarian, who weighed and counted everything for years!
Did I mention I was also a 6 cups of coffee a day gal who never slept and was TOTALLY miserable. As I said- 'reformed'. Yep, as of today.

Vibe- I am hard on myself and have a 'parent type' voice that often pipes up in my head and says things like- 'don't do this, don't do that, try harder, that wasn't good enough.... Etc!' Hence my former binge eating/ excersising problem. I have absolutely had enough of this voice being the primary in my thought life (that pops up especially when I am tired), so I am counteracting this by practising mindfulness, and focussing on what I have achieved! This requires the most effort out of everything on my part. But I think it may be one of the keys to living optimally for me!
I need to be the best person I can be to myself right now, regardless of all circumstances, especially to myself.
Xxxxxx

### Tjcam1 — 2013-03-10T05:34:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79903>

Hi everyone,
Thanks Jack for your thoughts! I absolutely have been driven in the past of what other people have thought/said what I should do with my life versus my own intuition and heart. Hopefully I can work in changing this over the next while!

To make things easier I'm breaking up this journal into 5 parts:
Song for the day, 'Vibe' (my inner thought life), sleep, food and training!

Song for the day: Ben Harper - With My Own Two Hands -
[www.youtube.com/watch?v=aEnfy9qfdaU](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aEnfy9qfdaU)

Try to watch this without being slightly touched! I dare ya!

'Vibe' ( thought processes)- Have SUCKED for the most part this week! Does anyone else out there have an inner 'parent' voice that just sometimes just nags you constantly? I have found this voice to be louder than normal this week- "could do this better, shouldn't have done that, that wasn't good enough etc" these challenges can work to my benefit I know, but often it's just irritating! I also think this sets me back, and is definitely bought on by lack of sleep!
My goal is to be a lot better with mindfulness and looking at achievements ( no matter how small) this week!

Sleep- was not awesome this week! My twin girls had terrible gastro, and so I was up most nights tending to them or worrying about them which couldn't be helped! They are improving wonderfully now in which I hope my sleep will too!

Food- eggs, prawns, oysters, tuna, coconut oil, bacon, avocado, nori, onion, chicken, and the list continues.....
Big achievement for me was throwing my stevia down the sink and having 1 coffee! This doesn't sound like much but I used to be a chronic sweet tooth, 6 cups of joe a day gal! I also haven't counted a calorie or weighed a morsel which is HUGE as I was an avid counter before now- 3 habits have already changed!

Training- cold showers all week for at least 10 minutes. This is not much I know, I hope though its an ok beginning?
4 weight training sessions incorporating HIIT and sprints! I never thought I wold enjoy a cld shower but I am totally addicted to it and the freshness in my body!

Also thanks to a tip from Inger I used seaweed on my face las night! Totally awesome facial ladies!!!

### Inger — 2013-03-10T07:56:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79908>

Nice song Tjcam1, and welcome! You are doing so well...!

Instead of listening to this stupid "nagging voice".. live 100% each moment, I bet it will help! To put my focus on the beautiful things, and always keep my eyes on where I am going helps me.. Life is NOW... You are so blessed to have those little sweet twin girls!

### Tjcam1 — 2013-03-10T13:17:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-from-average-to-awesome-down-under.6214/post-79928>

Thanks Inger! I will certinally apply this advice! Xx

# Thread: Thyroid lab interpretations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### diane — 2013-03-07T03:49:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79412>

I'm looking at my thyroid labs and I think I'm seeing what's going on, but this is all new to me.

TSH 2.68 mIU/L(.35-5)
FT3 4.4 pmol/L (2.6-5.7)
FT4 11 pmol/L (12-22)
RT3 13.4 ng/dL (9.2 - 24.1)

If I convert my ft# to ng/dL I get 56.6 and that gives me a FT3/RT3 ratio of 42, which is over the 20 minimum.

Because T4 is low and T3 is mid range - it looks to me like my issue is I"m not generating enough T4, and possibly some issue in converting to T3. I started upping my iodine (seaweed) today, as I already eat a lot of fish, to help with conversion to T3. (read BG 12 last night).

Any other thoughts are welcome!

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-07T03:53:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79415>

youre not too bad.........TSH is a bit higher than I like but your FT3 is not bad......you need the photoelectric effect and epi paleo Rx pushed hard and cut bad water......you are on your way Diane. I just got to convince you being naked is cool. LOL.

### diane — 2013-03-07T04:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79421>

Excellent - that's what I thought - not too bad. I feel like the key is in the ignition and it's trying to over. I'm just waiting for the engine to start.

I sleep naked - that's a start! LOL. Just need to find a private place to get outside naked.

### Destiny — 2013-03-07T14:00:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thyroid-lab-interpretations.6216/post-79450>

> [diane said:](/goto/post?id=79421)
>
> Excellent - that's what I thought - not too bad. I feel like the key is in the ignition and it's trying to over. I'm just waiting for the engine to start.
>
> I sleep naked - that's a start! LOL. Just need to find a private place to get outside naked.

A private place is an issue for me too. What about itsy bitsy bikini? Does it have to be naked???

# Thread: CT impact on individuals with CF?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2013-03-08T02:33:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79572>

I have searched the internet the best I could, but was not able to find any data regarding the impact/experience of CT on individuals that have cystic fibrosis.

The reason I ask is my girlfriend is a mid 20's female who was diagnosed with cystic fibrosis at 3 months old. My concern is her body, especially lungs and digestive system, have degraded significantly from the disease. Her current lung function (Pulminary Function Test) is ~50-55% of "ideal" for her age/height/body type.

Does anyone happen to know of other cases of cystic fibrosis patients who implemented CT?

Any recommendations for modifying the default protocol to cater to a cystic fibrosis patient? (Example, due to the lower lung capacity she will have difficulty with holding her breath for the initial face dips. Should she phase in another method?)

Would there be an alternate skin tempurature to stop at instead of 50-55 deg. for a "normal" person?

She is definitely going to discuss this with her doctors before beginning, but I am concerned that they will tell her to stay clear of it sheerly due to the fact they know nothing about it. Any input would be appreciated!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-08T02:53:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79575>

Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.

She also might want to consider d-ribose.

### BJK77 — 2013-03-08T14:57:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79649>

Hi Sjoshua!

Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:

[http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2013-03-08T15:26:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79652>

### sjoshua — 2013-03-09T02:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-79758>

Hey all, thanks for the great welcome and quick replies!

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79575)
>
> Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
> I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.
>
> She also might want to consider d-ribose.

Nonchalant: Ok, that sounds good. We will start small and keep a close eye on everything as we ramp up the time and transition to ice packs/baths. She is going to begin phasing in the CT next week. I felt bad that she was going to be doing it alone, and decided to jump in head first myself and started doing cold showers yesterday... WOW! they are invigurating, but I like it. I think I am going to start cold baths when she starts face dunks.

Regarding your second note, I haven't read much about d-ribose yet... could you point me to a good article for some background information and practical uses (such as this)?

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=79649)
>
> Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:
>
> [http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

BJK77: Great article! That is actually one of the regular treatments she uses: an inhaled saline solution that was actually developed by CF patients that realized how beneficial the ocean air was to their breathing. We do get down to the ocean when we can, but being ~45 minutes away makes it difficult more often. Moving even closer to the ocean is definitely a hope for the future. This past summer we relocated from upstate, NY out here to San Jose, CA for the beneficial effects of being in a more temperate, beautiful climate.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)
>
> Wait til you read the QB bipedalism blog........ties into this whole thing.

Jack, I just stumbled onto your site indirectly through Mark's Daily Apple about a week ago and I can't get enough! Thank you for providing such a logical, detailed analysis on so many important aspects of understanding how these "human machines" we occupy function. I haven't came across the QB bipedalism blog as of yet though... is that still forthcoming? Either way, I am looking forward to reading how these tie together.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-12T00:08:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80103>

Good thought on the washcloth, that my be an even better way for her to phase in since it won't hinder breathing as much. Your note on d-ribose: I am not familiar with this. Could you please point me to a good article or source so I can learn what it does and specifically how it could help here? Thanks!

BJK77: That is a great article! Thanks for pointing it out. From what I understand, it was actually a surfer that originally came up with the idea, but one of the treatments in my GF's normal regimen is an inhaled saline solution to imitate the salty air. Although we are in San Jose, sadly it is still ~45 minutes from the ocean so we can't get there as often as would be beneficial. Some day we hope to be closer

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)

Jack: I stumbled across your site second-hand through a link off of Mark's Daily Apple about a week ago, and haven't been able to get enough! Our society would be in a much better place than putting the Rx bandaid on everything.

As far as the blog post you mentioned above, I haven't come across that one yet. Is it filed under another name, or is it still forthcoming? Thanks in advance!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80529>

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-14T21:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-80540>

I haven't seen any posts come through from you sjoshua that needed to be approved. At least not today or yesterday. Maybe that darn internet monster ate them! The same one that keeps eating Jack's webinars! LOL

### sjoshua — 2013-03-20T19:10:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81198>

Ok, one more time. let's hope it posts this time!!

> [Nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=79575)
>
> Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
> I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.
>
> She also might want to consider d-ribose.

Nonchalant, thank you for the recommendations. We have been doing ice water-soaked wash cloths placed over her face (with a second folded over her forehead at the same time) for 15 minutes every other day since last Wednesday for a nice, easy phase in.

Additionally, I did some research and picked up some D-Ribose, which she has been loading on at 5g 2-3x/day since CT started.

So far things are going well. I have a skin thermometer and have been measuring her before and immediately after temps on both cheek and forehead; by the end (at at intervals throughout) we are keeping her face temps at around 64-67. Do you think we should try to go colder, or not yet?

At one point last night about 4-5 minutes in, she said her temples were too cold and she had to take a break for ~15 seconds; her forehead temp at that point had been 64.

> [BJK77 said:](/goto/post?id=79649)
>
> Hi Sjoshua!
>
> Your post made me remember an article I read a few days ago about using the ocean and the salty air to help open the lungs of those with CF. I just did a quick google search and found the link:
>
> [http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

BJK77: Thanks for the welcome, and also great article! I have seen it before but its always nice to see when other people are knowledgeable about CF, since it is quite rare. Although we are in San Jose, we are still about ~30-45 minutes drive form the ocean... We do get down there when possible!

I believe that a CF surfer discovered how beneficial the salty sea air was, and they have since made it into an inhaled Rx. Using a saline solution in a nebulizer, she can experience a similar effect on a daily basis

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=79652)

>
> [http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563\_162-6840072.html](http://www.cbsnews.com/8301-18563_162-6840072.html)

BJK77: Thanks for the welcome, and also great article! I have seen it before but its always nice to see when other people are knowledgeable about CF, since it is quite rare. Although we are in San Jose, we are still about ~30-45 minutes drive form the ocean... We do get down there when possible!

I believe that a CF surfer discovered how beneficial the salty sea air was, and they have since made it into an inhaled Rx. Using a saline solution in a nebulizer, she can experience a similar effect on a daily basis

Quote Originally Posted by Nonchalant View Post
Hi Sjoshua, welcome!
I think 5-15 second dunks would be just fine, just do more than one.

She also might want to consider d-ribose.
Nonchalant, thank you for the recommendations. We have been doing ice water-soaked wash cloths placed over her face (with a second folded over her forehead at the same time) for 15 minutes every other day since last Wednesday for a nice, easy phase in.

Additionally, I did some research and picked up some D-Ribose, which she has been loading on at 5g 2-3x/day since CT started.

So far things are going well. I have a skin thermometer and have been measuring her before and immediately after temps on both cheek and forehead; by the end (at at intervals throughout) we are keeping her face temps at around 64-67. Do you think we should try to go colder, or not yet?

At one point last night about 4-5 minutes in, she said her temples were too cold and she had to take a break for ~15 seconds; her forehead temp at that point had been 64.

Thanks to Patty Cakes recommend, I reached out to the [support@jackkruse.com](mailto:support@jackkruse.com) e-mail for help about my post. They thought I may have just been posting too long of a response and it was getting flagged as spam. No fixes for it, so I will just have to post more, shorter replies in the future I guess

To be completely honest, she wants almost nothing to do with the CT, and at this point she only thinks it makes her cold. I have explained some of the "why" behind it, but the reason she is doing it at all is because we are amidst a 30-day experiment during which she is letting me control her diet, exercise, journaling, relaxation, etc. aspects of daily life, to see if the lifestyle ideas I have for her are more or less beneficial than the boiler plate stuff she hears from her doctors.

### nonchalant — 2013-03-21T18:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81326>

When I first started the CT protocol, I only did face dunks for about 15 seconds, and only repeated them 2 or 3 times each day. Can she do 15 seconds in ice water? Even if only once per day? She may find it surprising. Better than the face cloths. When I first did it, I was a bit scared, but I was amazed that I didn't feel the cold very much. It's like the nerves in my face just shut off the cold sensation. The second and third dunks, however, felt much colder than the first.

Sorry that part of your 30 days was affected by posting issues.

### sjoshua — 2013-03-25T18:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-impact-on-individuals-with-cf.6230/post-81719>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=81326)
>
> When I first started the CT protocol, I only did face dunks for about 15 seconds, and only repeated them 2 or 3 times each day. Can she do 15 seconds in ice water? Even if only once per day? She may find it surprising. Better than the face cloths. When I first did it, I was a bit scared, but I was amazed that I didn't feel the cold very much. It's like the nerves in my face just shut off the cold sensation. The second and third dunks, however, felt much colder than the first.
>
> Sorry that part of your 30 days was affected by posting issues.

nonchalant: Thanks again for the feedback. This week I was planning to switch her over to face dunks, but last night she made it apparent that she did not want to move past the wash cloths, so I am not going to force things further.

The goal of my 30 days was to put as many positive concepts in place regarding her health, nutrition, fitness, etc. I hope that she takes away as many of them as possible, but I know that maintaining CT is something someone can really only decide for themself. It was the wild card I threw in

At leasth the take-away from this is I have been enjoying my cold showers daily; they are definitely getting easier and are a really great way to wake up in the morning! I plan to continue them, but at this point I am 99% confident that my girlfriend will not be sticking to the CT after the experiment ends.

# Thread: Adjusting to daylight savings time

### diane — 2013-03-08T04:10:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-79590>

I'm assuming that daylight savings time can throw the circadian rhythms out of sync. Does anyone have thoughts on how to compensate? I'm wondering if using melatonin for a couple of days will help, similar to a jet lag situation. I know I always feel like crap for a few days after.

### ashryn — 2013-03-08T11:01:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-79620>

Not sure that it would throw the rhythms out of whack.. It's really us adjusting to the fact that we had imposed time on out lifestyle.. Really, if there were no clocks and starting times, we'd adjust our sleep wake cycles to the light anyway..

### mrc — 2020-11-01T21:55:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adjusting-to-daylight-savings-time.6231/post-293187>

[https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1322919465693122561](https://twitter.com/DrJackKruse/status/1322919465693122561)
"Standard time is equivalent to living and eating things grown locally under the sun.
Daylight savings time is like eating Keto/Paleo/Vegan snacks made in a blue light lab with no protection of photosynthetic algorithms nature provides.

11/6/16
Daylight savings time CREATES a circadian mismatch you need to hack. I do it every year. DST give us a 17% rise in fatal traffic accidents
Between 1986 and 1995, fatal traffic accidents rose 17% the Monday following the switch to Daylight Saving Time.

Source: University of Colorado. Want some more? Over $9,000,000 in electricity cost due to DST and this tells you why the government allows DST to persist. It is about money. And that electric power grid causes circadian disruption because of its artificial light and the electric power grids electric and magnetic fields.

A 2006 report found DST led to a 1 percent overall rise in residential electricity use in Indiana, costing the the state an extra $9 million. Researchers said that cost could be even higher in other regions of the U.S.
Source: National Bureau of Economic Research. More? There is a 68% increase in days lost due to workplace injury

From 1983 to 2006, there was a 68% increase in lost working days due to workplace injury on the Monday following the switch to DST.
Source: American Psychological Association. More? 25% increase in heart attack risk
A 2014 study showed that the hour of sleep lost when switching to DST can increase your risk of a heart attack by 25%.

Hello
I have joined the Optimal Reset the best I could but I have a question:

I have had problems for years with hypothyroidism and increased Erfa Thyroid with Â½ grain to 4Â½ grain in october or november last year (I cant remember the exact time). Below are my testresults:

July 2012 4 grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 1,00 (1,2-2,8)
T4 17,8 0 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

December 2012 4Â½ grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 2,01 (1,2-2,8)
T4 = 19,8 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

Febuary 2013 4Â½ grain Erfa Thyroid
T3 = 2,98 (1,2-2,8)
T4 = 25,1 (10-26)
TSH = 0,01 (0,3-4)

I have only changed 2 things since the bloodtest in december:
1\. the 25th january started drinking 1Â½ liter of water with seaweed every day. I put it in the refrigreator in the evening.
2\. The 25th january I cut my BAB to HAB

### Inger — 2013-03-12T08:16:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80161>

No Jasmin, I do not filter my water because we have our own well here, it comes from 30 meter deep. It is not a well that flows up from the ground itself, which would be the best.. but it gets pumped up with a pump. It is not filtered though and nothing added either, and it tastes great! I take care to always let the water flow quite a bit before I fill my glass jugs for the day. I want it fresh from the earth, not water that have been stuck in the tubes for long.

I just realized the water gets a way stronger "taste of sun" when it put it outside in a glass bowl instead of a jug!!! way different! I bet it is because it has a large surface to suck up the sunbeams directly, not through a glass... told me something about glass isolating pretty well..... from the goodness of the sun.. wow it really was a difference! So I need to buy some more big glass bowls like this...

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

About seaweeds.
I made some Dulce seaweed water the other day first time ever.. Dulce is very high Iodine. I realized yesterday I have a weird bump on my one finger... I assume it is from a bit too much Iodine? I got me some Brazil nuts.. and ate a handful yesterday after work..lol I will drink some saltwater today too. I think it is possible to get detox with seaweeds too, without doubt! It is good to check the Iodine content of the different brands because it varies huge. It could be a issue if one is drinking much from the high Iodine ones...

Jasmin, if no possibility to buy seaweeds from cleaner sources I do think also Baltic Sea seaweeds could be healthy, but I would do that only if no other option. But I bet it is better that nothing! I do eat fish from Baltic Sea too once in a while... I do try to limit it though.

### Jasmin — 2013-03-14T10:33:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80479>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=80167)
>
> Kelp has very high Iodine.. I guess Nori too and Dulce.. it also depends on where they grow. But your body will tell you sooner or later so just listen to it and adjust as needed, that is how I do and it works great.

I have found the iodine level in different types of seaweed:
In 100 gram there is:
Nori = 5 mg
Wakame = 15 mg
Dulse = 20 mg
Arame = 60 mg
Combu =340 mg (I know this level looks like it is a joke )

The level can be different from season and where it is harvested.

I dont know what to listen for if I am getting too much iodine - do you know the signs?

I know that some warn against iodine if one has truble with the metabolism but I havent found out why yet. But if Jack says that seaweed can get me off the medicine then I believe him

Though I'm wondering now if the headache I used to get was actually dehydration.

### Patty Cakes — 2013-03-14T21:17:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80538>

When I get too much iodine I break out in a horrible rash. But now I'm also wondering if I was just having a bromide flush. I had a rash on my back for 7 months that was awful. It was oozing and I thought I had a hot tub rash. It may have started out that way but back then I was taking about 50mg of iodine/day. At the time I thought the hot tub rash just kept on perpetuating but now looking back I'm pretty sure it was just excess iodine pushing out the bromide. I never want to go there again!

### nonchalant — 2013-03-18T02:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-80924>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=80161)
>
> I just realized the water gets a way stronger "taste of sun" when it put it outside in a glass bowl instead of a jug!!! way different! I bet it is because it has a large surface to suck up the sunbeams directly, not through a glass... told me something about glass isolating pretty well..... from the goodness of the sun.. wow it really was a difference!

Oh, this is great!! I had access to well water this weekend, and so I put some in a soup bowl out in the sun. It really changes the flavor! Makes it "mellow", in my opinion. I tried it several times, in the course of a day, and found that just a few minutes in the sun, less than 5 min, is enough, if the sun is high. The water would still be cool, but remarkably different than it tasted a few minutes before. There would be small bubbles on the surface of the bowl.

I also left some out for a couple hours, and I actually didn't like the taste. The next day was cloudy, and though I tried it again, I didn't get the same effect. The water tasted much the same as from the tap. Sun water needs sun!

Great discovery, Inger. Thanks!

### Melanie Procter — 2014-05-10T10:07:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/could-it-be-the-seaweed.6234/post-126164>

I am having trouble finding a good seaweed company that is a one stop shop. I have looked at some of these but they don't have a comprehensive line of seaweeds. I would love if someone made a product and blended together these seaweeds. I clipped this from Jack's blog Brain/Gut 6
When eating non ocean bearing fats/protein consider adding Sea Veggies to the recipe:

A. **Irish Moss** (chondrus crispus, carrageen) is full of electrolyte minerals — calcium, magnesium, sodium and potassium. Its mucilaginous compounds help you detoxify, boost metabolism and strengthen hair, skin and nails. In Eastern medicine it is traditionally used for a low sex drive because it helps support T3 levels to convert LDL to progesterone and not to cortisol.

B. **Wakame** (alaria, undaria) is a high-protein, high calcium seaweed, with carotenes, iron and vitamin C. It is used in Chinese medicine for hair growth and luster, and for skin tone because it optimizes thyroid function.

C. **Kelp** (laminaria) contains vitamins the fat soluble vitamins A, B, E, D and K in high quantity, and is a major source of vitamin C from the sea. It also happens to be rich in many minerals found in land based plants. This is why it made [my top ten supplement list](http://www.jackkruse.com/what-are-the-top-ten-paleo-supplements/) so long ago in the blog series. Kelp proteins are high quality and present in abundance for a sea plant. It is a brown marine plant, kelp contains sodium alginate (algin), an element that helps remove radioactive particles (think Fukishima Iodine) and heavy metals from the body. Kelp can works as a blood purifier, relieves arthritis stiffness, and promotes adrenal, pituitary and thyroid health. Kelp’s natural iodine can normalize thyroid-related disorders like obesity and muscle fatigue seen in fibromyalgia cases. **Clinical pearl:**people with herpes virus of any type: It is a demulcent that helps eliminate herpes outbreaks after they have occurred. Kelp is nutrient rich and a small amount gives a large clinical result often.

D. **Hijiki** is a mineral-rich, high-fiber seaweed, with 20% protein, vitamin A, carotenes and calcium. Hijiki has the **most calcium** of any sea green, 1400mg per 100 grams of dry weight.

E. **Kombu** (laminaria digitata, setchelli, horsetail kelp), has a long tradition as a Japanese delicacy with reported nutritional healing value. Natural healers use it is a decongestant for excess mucous and to helps normalize blood pressure. Kombu has abundant iodine, carotenes, B, C, D and E vitamins, minerals like calcium, magnesium, potassium, silica, iron and zinc, and the powerful skin healing nutrient germanium, which is a pretty rare element in the human diet. Kombu is a meaty, high-protein seaweed. It is higher in natural mineral salts than most other seaweeds. I often add a strip of kombu to my bone broths and seafood broths.

F. **Nori** (porphyra, laver) is a red sea plant with a sweet, meaty taste when dried. It contains nearly 50% balanced, assimilable protein, higher than any other sea plant. Nori’s fiber makes it popular for sushi wrapping. I do not eat any rice because its miRNA directly affects our DNA expression. Nori is rich in all the carotenes, calcium, iodine, iron, and phosphorus.

G. **Sea Palm** (Postelsia Palmaeformis), American arame, grows only on the Pacific Coast of North America by Oregon and Washington State. It has a honeyed, salty taste that makes it tasty vegetable. I like it with my cauliflower rice recipe form the [e-cookbook](http://www.jackkruse.com/optimized-cooking/), or as a summer or autumn salad topping.

H. **Bladderwrack** is loaded with vitamin K and it is an excellent adrenal adaptogen and it helps sensitize us to insulin to because of its K2 like effects. It is often used today by Native American cultures broths and in sauna/steam baths for degenerative arthritis and inflammatory joint conditions.

I. **Dulse** (palmaria palmata), a red sea plant, very rich in iron. The last two sea veggies are my favorites because they have the most iodine of any plants on our planet. I was made aware of this sea vegetable when I was researching the Geritol solution for global warming. It also has abundant protein, and vitamin A. What really makes it a specially balanced nutrient for a lactovegetarian paleo diet is that it contains 300 times more iodine and 50 times more iron than wheat products. Tests on dulse show antiviral action against the herpes virus.

J.**Arame** (Eisenia bycyclis), is one of the ocean’s richest sources of iodine. It often contains **more iodine than seafood does**! Because of its high iodine content it can have major effects on women’s progesterone to estradiol ratio’s naturally. If you are estrogen dominant you might consider using this as become a staple in your broths and recipes. Many natural healers use arame to help reduce breast and uterine fibroids, excessive bleeding, fibrocystic disease of the breasts, and ovarian cysts. It is also quite helpful in cases of PCOS with acne and excessive facial hair. It also contains a lot fat soluble vitamins and phytohormones, to normalize peri menopausal and menopausal symptoms. Arame is often associated with soft wrinkle-free skin turgor and tends to enhances hair growth and make your hair have an incredible sheen. The reason is because this veggie increase your free T3 to a dramatic degree when it is used regularly in your diet.

Yes.......T3 has three iodine atoms

"most of the moisturizers women use are estrogen creators and by definition......iodine depleters........when your Prog/E2 levels are off you iodine usually sucks.......and this results in high LDL and low free T3. Low T3 means a low 2 hydroxylase and 16 hydroxylase issue for estrogens.......this further makes your prog/E2 worse."

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T15:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79795>

For two years, I have been drinking Fiji water, Apollinaris, & WF 365 brand water, which is just Crystal Geyser sourced from a spring in the Appalachian mountains that is completely untouched by man above the source.

When I'm pregnant, I can't drink still water. I don't know why. It must be sparkling. My pregnant self would accept club soda, but not still water, and strongly preferred Apollinaris.

When I'm not, I can drink still water. I always look for "that taste" - the taste of the water I grew up on, which was from a well in Georgia. While little else was right about my upbringing, health-wise, the water was clean & tasted good. When I first tasted real spring water, I knew what I had been missing ever since moving to "city water" - the minerals! I started buying ConcenTrace and adding it to my water till it had "that taste."

I started buying a lot of FIji, but was uncomfortable with flying it around the planet when the people in Fiji don't even really get much of it. Now I buy just one liter/wk of that and use it whenever I'm upstairs. I think that's probably a good move in an additional way - the app someone posted from Gerolsteiner's website that compares mineral content of waters shows that FIji is very, very high in silicon (that's why it feels so smooth), but little else. Apollinaris is leagues higher in Mg & many other trace minerals, higher even than the Gerolsteiner that the app was made to push!

The CG water also has a good taste (good indicator of mineral content), but I still add ConcenTrace, b/c my whole family drinks it as our main water source, and I know we are all mineral deprived. They don't all yet eat as much seafood as I do, and I know our soils are so depleted that even organic food & water no longer have enough minerals.

I was thinking about getting a Berkey filter, top of the line with fluoride filter, too, and using it for our tap water (it would end up at $.05/gallon), which comes from the Chattahoochee River (which starts as a mountain stream in the Appalachians, but then goes through Lake Lanier and gets boated in & floats around, probably for years, till it comes out through the dam!), but I think I've decided to stay with CG for now. $.80/gallon when I buy it by the case at WF.

BTW, I'm still dehydrated, lol. Horrible Bun/creatinine ratio & getting worse (has gotten worse every lab I've taken for the 1.5yrs since starting the LR), wake up every morning with bloody sinus stuff coming out my nose (this started around the time I started the LR), & massive water retention across my whole body that fluctuates up and down 10lb in just a few days, often without me understanding what the cause is.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T17:20:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79802>

All water naturally has fluoride. It's an element. Some has more than others, depending on the mineral content of the surrounding area, but it's a \*trace\* mineral in natural water, in \*trace\* amounts. The question is, does it have industrial waste (sodium fluoride) added to it? That's the real hazard.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T19:50:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79822>

Wait - is someone trying to say there is water somewhere on earth that is naturally occurring and has \*no\* fluoride in it? Granted, I haven't read every single word on this forum, but I missed that.

From wikipedia: "Fresh water supplies generally contain between **0.01–0.3 ppm**, whereas the ocean contains between **1.2 and 1.5 ppm**. (Not that anyone here is drinking ocean water, but YKWIM.) ... In some locations, the fresh water contains dangerously high levels of fluoride, leading to serious health problems."

Obviously in natural water that naturally contains excessive levels, you have a problem. But is anyone saying they have a source that \*naturally\*, without any kind of filtering, has <.01ppm fluoride in it?

Don't forget to compare apples to apples. Governing bodies almost always allow groups to round down to zero if they have below a certain amount. Filtered municipal water that is smack dab in the middle of "normal" for fresh water, say at .08mg/L (which is .08ppm), could easily be rounded down and reported as zero, when it actually has just as much as most natural sources of water.

### MamaGrok — 2013-03-09T19:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/spring-water-observations.6236/post-79824>

I just checked that website, and it appears to be reporting almost every non-fluoridated municipal supply as "zero." That just means they're rounding down. They're simply not getting specific to that degree. All natural water, to my knowledge, has fluoride in it, in the range given at wikipedia. I'd be very interested in information showing that there is any naturally occurring water with fluoride at a significantly lower concentration.

I woke up around 11pm, got up brushed my teeth, popped in my OSA Sleep device, and applied my Pg BHRT crawled into our bed by 1115 and was out in under 10mins. when I woke up - to my alarm - it was like amazing how I felt. WOW is all I can say. I did some serious grounding yesterday - on the way to work, at lunch, on the way home, and playing with the kids and the dog in the back yard.

going to try to do the same thing today... and get in the CT Tank as well..

What I can't figure out is if the good sleeping is a combination of several things or just one thing. We've been using the magnetico sleep pad for almost a month, and I've been focusing on grounding for almost an entire month. I'm not to the point that this is an every night occurrence but its happening more often than not.

### Jonathin — 2013-03-08T22:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/best-sleep-ever.6237/post-79703>

It seems that the PPP does more than make ribose and reduce glutathione. Maybe increased endogenous hormones are kicking in.

"Enzymes that function primarily in the reductive direction utilize the NADP+/NADPH cofactor pair as co-factors
as opposed to oxidative enzymes that utilize the NAD+/NADH cofactor pair. The reactions of fatty acid
biosynthesis and **steroid biosynthesis utilize large amounts of NADPH**. As a consequence, cells of the liver,
adipose tissue, adrenal cortex, testis and lactating mammary gland have high levels of the PPP enzymes. In fact
**30% of the oxidation of glucose in the liver occurs via the PPP.** Additionally, erythrocytes utilize the reactions of the
PPP to generate large amounts of NADPH used in the reduction of glutathione (see below). The conversion of
ribonucleotides to deoxyribonucleotides (through the action of ribonucleotide reductase) requires NADPH as the
electron source, therefore, any rapidly proliferating cell needs large quantities of NADPH."
\-themedicalbiochemistrypage.org-
